#770 lora driver: SX1276 reports incorrect RSSIIssue #13036 micropython/lora, RSSI for Hope Radio devicesclosed1.762DUPLICATE
Both issues describe the exact same problem: the SX1276 LoRa...

Both issues describe the exact same problem: the SX1276 LoRa driver incorrectly calculates RSSI values using a PA_BOOST-based offset (157 vs 164) instead of a frequency-based offset. Issue #770 provides detailed analysis showing the formula is invalid and should condition on RF frequency > 525MHz. Issue #13036 questions the same formula's correctness for Hope Radio devices (RF96/RFM96W). The affected code, symptoms, and root cause are identical. Issue #770 is a continuation of discussion #12989, and #13036 is the original report of the same bug.

Action: Close issue #13036 as a duplicate of issue #770, which contains more detailed analysis and the correct fix direction (conditioning on RF frequency rather than PA_BOOST status).

Issue #11181 reports the same behavior ('when I use the wifi connect method with a wrong password in a network which I logged in recently with the right password, the login seems to be successful!!!') and explicitly asks 'WHY? WTF happens here?'. The candidate issue #11181 fully addresses the problem described in the query, making it a duplicate.

Action: Close issue #971 as a duplicate of issue #11181, or vice versa depending on which has more detail. Since #11181 contains the same bug report plus additional context (documentation mismatch for status('rssi')), consider keeping #11181 open and closing #971 as duplicate, or merge the reports if #11181 is newer.

#2028 super() appears not to work with multiple inheritanceIssue #3205 In case of multiple inheritance, super() might not find some parent methodsclosed1.757DUPLICATE
Both issues report the same fundamental bug: super() not cor...

Both issues report the same fundamental bug: super() not correctly implementing Method Resolution Order (MRO) in multiple inheritance scenarios. Issue #2028 shows super() skipping intermediate classes (C.__init__ is omitted in D(B,C)), while issue #3205 shows super() failing to find parent methods entirely (AttributeError). Both stem from the same root cause in the super() implementation in py/objtype.c. The concepts, affected components, and underlying problem are identical - they are different manifestations of the same MRO/super() bug.

Action: Close issue #3205 as a duplicate of #2028. Both describe the same super() MRO bug in multiple inheritance. If a fix has been merged for either issue, ensure both are closed accordingly.

#1037 aiohttp: ws: frame not fully received/read if largeIssue #1012 Fix attached for aiohttp's aiohttp_ws.py partial read on ESP32closed1.737DUPLICATE
Both issues describe the exact same root cause: MicroPython'...

Both issues describe the exact same root cause: MicroPython's asyncio.Stream.read(n) does not guarantee reading all requested bytes in a single call, causing WebSocket payload truncation. Issue #1037 reports the symptom (truncated large payloads), while issue #1012 provides the complete fix implementing a loop to read remaining bytes until the full payload is received. The fix in #1012 directly resolves the problem described in #1037.

Action: Close issue #1037 as duplicate of #1012, since #1012 contains the complete solution that addresses the reported bug.

#2724 ESP8266: Extremely bad clock accuracyPR #2726 ESP8266: Improved time keepingclosed1.654DUPLICATE
The PR #2726 directly addresses the issue #2724. The issue r...

The PR #2726 directly addresses the issue #2724. The issue reports extremely bad clock accuracy on ESP8266 (1 second drift every 30-80 seconds) caused by relying on the RTC calibration via system_rtc_clock_cali_proc(). The PR explicitly states it implements 'access to non-RTC system clock, which is more stable than built-in RTC' and 'Modified general time function to use system clock instead of RTC'. This matches the proposed solution in the issue. The PR also includes improved RTC tracking code, addressing the root cause mentioned in the issue.

Action: Close issue #2724 as resolved by PR #2726. The PR implements the exact solution proposed in the issue (switching to system clock for better accuracy) and includes additional improvements to RTC handling.

#18076 RP2: ADC pins does not work properly in XIAO RP2350PR #18443 lib/pico-sdk: Update pico-sdk submodule to version 2.2.0.closed1.632DUPLICATE
Issue #18076 reports that ADC pins don't work on XIAO RP2350...

Issue #18076 reports that ADC pins don't work on XIAO RP2350 due to an outdated pico-sdk that lacks the ADC_BASE_PIN and NUM_ADC_CHANNELS macros required by recent MicroPython ADC validation logic. The PR #18443 explicitly updates the pico-sdk submodule to version 2.2.0, which includes the necessary board definition changes (as referenced in the issue's link to the pico-sdk commit c88bd53a). The PR description confirms 'Compatibility updates for RP2350-based devices (including Seeed XIAO RP2350) with critical hardware macro definitions' and mentions 'enhanced ADC/DAC functionality'. This PR directly resolves the root cause identified in the issue.

Action: Close issue #18076 as resolved by PR #18443. The PR update to pico-sdk v2.2.0 includes the required ADC_BASE_PIN and NUM_ADC_CHANNELS macros that fix the ADC functionality on XIAO RP2350.

#13385 Compiling with FROZEN_MANIFEST fails to build mpy-cross if it hasn't been built separately firstPR #5760 py/mkrules.mk: Add $(MPY_CROSS) as a dependencyclosed1.609DUPLICATE
Issue #13385 reports that building with FROZEN_MANIFEST fail...

Issue #13385 reports that building with FROZEN_MANIFEST fails to build mpy-cross if it hasn't been built separately first, causing a linker error with undefined reference to `mp_qstr_frozen_const_pool`. PR #5760 directly addresses this by adding `$(MPY_CROSS)` as an order-only prerequisite in py/mkrules.mk, which ensures mpy-cross is automatically built when needed. The PR description explicitly states it 'removes the need to remember to build ../../mpy-cross/mpy-cross by hand on a clean checkout', which is exactly the problem described in the issue. The fix targets the same root cause (build system dependency issue) and resolves the same symptoms.

Action: Close issue #13385 as resolved by PR #5760

#400 OSError: -40Issue #396 OSError: [Errno 5] EIO, urequests errorclosed1.596DUPLICATE
Both issues report the same underlying problem: HTTPS reques...

Both issues report the same underlying problem: HTTPS requests failing on ESP8266 with SSL handshake errors. Issue #400 reports 'OSError: -40' while issue #396 reports 'ssl_handshake_status: -40' followed by 'OSError: [Errno 5] EIO'. The error code -40 is the same root cause (SSL/TLS handshake failure due to cipher suite incompatibility between axtls and modern servers). Both affect the same components (esp8266/network, extmod/ussl) and the same code paths in urequests.py. The synopsis in #400 explicitly identifies this as the axtls cipher suite incompatibility issue, which is the root cause of both reports.

Action: Close issue #396 as duplicate of #400, as #400 has a more complete analysis and summary identifying the root cause (axtls cipher suite incompatibility). Keep #400 open as it contains the comprehensive diagnosis.

#797 tarfile-write issue with file/folder permissionsPR #799 python-stdlib/tarfile: Fixed permissions when adding to archive.merged1.581DUPLICATE
The PR #799 explicitly references and fixes issue #797. The ...

The PR #799 explicitly references and fixes issue #797. The PR description states 'This change fixes issue https://github.com/micropython/micropython-lib/issues/797'. Both items address the same bug: tarfile-write creating archives with incorrect zero permissions because os.stat()[0] returns file type bits instead of permission bits. The PR implements the fix by defaulting to 0o755 for directories and 0o644 for files, which directly resolves the problem described in the issue.

Action: Close issue #797 as resolved by PR #799

The PR fixes exactly this problem by sanitizing non-C-identifier characters (including dots) in compiled module names before generating C code. The testing section even uses the exact same command pattern from the issue (-s 'test.mpy').

The PR #814 in micropython-lib directly implements the featu...

The PR #814 in micropython-lib directly implements the feature request described in Issue #13540. The PR description explicitly references the issue ('See also [#13540]') and implements all the core proposals: moving password checking to Python, removing the special file transfer protocol, moving REPL data to binary websocket packages, and adding new features like file browser, mount, and mip support. The PR description even mentions the same proof-of-concept repository (felixdoerre/webreplv2) referenced in the issue.

Action: Close issue #13540 as resolved by PR #814. The PR implements the requested feature parity between webrepl and mpremote, including all the proposed changes to the protocol and new capabilities.

#400 OSError: -40Issue #8251 esp32 esp8266 urequestsclosed1.552DUPLICATE
Both items describe the exact same underlying problem: urequ...

Both items describe the exact same underlying problem: urequests failing with OSError -40 (or 40) on ESP8266 when attempting HTTPS connections, while working fine on ESP32. The query item (400) provides a detailed bug report with stack trace and specific error code, while the candidate item (8251) is a user question asking about the same phenomenon. The synopsis in the query item explicitly identifies the root cause (axtls SSL library incompatibility with modern TLS cipher suites on ESP8266). The candidate item directly addresses the same issue from a user's perspective, making it a duplicate that resolves the query.

Action: Close issue #400 as a duplicate of #8251, or close #8251 as a duplicate of #400 depending on which has better detail. Since #400 has more technical detail and a comprehensive summary, keep #400 and close #8251 as duplicate.

#15670 Binary operations on undefined variables crash the native emitter.PR #15723 py/runtime: Check for null objects coming from native functions.closed1.546DUPLICATE
The PR explicitly states 'This fixes issue #15670' in its de...

The PR explicitly states 'This fixes issue #15670' in its description. The issue describes a crash when performing binary operations on undefined variables in @micropython.native functions, and the PR provides a fix that converts this crash into a proper NameError exception. The reproduction code in the PR matches the issue exactly (a += 10 vs a += 0). The PR modifies the same code areas mentioned in the issue (py/runtime.c, specifically mp_binary_op).

Action: Close issue #15670 as resolved by PR #15723

#795 os-path causes build conflict with unix_ffi osPR #672 unix-ffi: Isolate packages from python-stdlib version of `os`closed1.531DUPLICATE
The issue describes a build conflict where adding `glob` wit...

The issue describes a build conflict where adding `glob` with `unix_ffi=True` causes a redefinition error because `glob` requires `os-path`, which in turn requires `os` without `unix_ffi`, conflicting with the `os` already required with `unix_ffi`. The PR explicitly addresses this by adding unix-ffi variants for `os-path` and `uu` so that when `glob` or `email.message` request these dependencies, the unix-ffi versions are installed instead of the python-stdlib versions. The PR description even mentions `glob` specifically as one of the packages that causes this issue. The fix proposed in the issue (adding `unix_ffi=True` to `os-path/manifest.py`) is exactly what the PR implements.

Action: Close issue #795 as resolved by PR #672. The PR directly addresses the build conflict described in the issue by providing unix-ffi variants for the affected packages.

#5095 [NRF] Are there any plans to support the NFC functionality of the nRF52..PR #5104 nrf/nfc: Add basic support for NFC tag 2.closed1.507DUPLICATE
The query (Issue #5095) explicitly asks about plans to expos...

The query (Issue #5095) explicitly asks about plans to expose the NFC slave functionality of nRF52 chips for pairing, sleep activation, and configuration. The candidate (PR #5104) directly implements this feature by adding a new NFC machine module that exposes NFC tag 2 support, including APIs to start/stop the NFC peripheral and set raw payloads. The PR addresses the exact functionality requested in the issue, making it a resolved duplicate.

Action: Close issue #5095 as resolved by PR #5104. The PR implements the requested NFC slave functionality for nRF52 devices.

#190 Typing modulePR #297 typing Add fake classes to typing module (Issue #190)closed1.499DUPLICATE
The PR #297 explicitly states 'Addresses issue #190' in its ...

The PR #297 explicitly states 'Addresses issue #190' in its description. The PR implements the requested feature by adding fake/stub typing classes to the typing module, which directly resolves the feature request in issue #190 to port typing module functionality to MicroPython. The synopsis and affected code match perfectly between the issue and the PR.

Action: Close issue #190 as resolved by PR #297

#16490 time.mktime() returns incorrect Unix timestamp on ESP32PR #5972 esp32/time: make utime conform to CPython and add SNTPclosed1.481DUPLICATE
The issue #16490 reports that time.mktime() returns incorrec...

The issue #16490 reports that time.mktime() returns incorrect Unix timestamps on ESP32 (returning dates from 1994 instead of 2024), which is caused by the ESP32 port using a non-standard epoch. The candidate PR #5972 explicitly addresses this by changing the Epoch to be the POSIX 1970 Epoch and making utime.localtime and utime.mktime conform to the CPython standard. The PR description directly references fixing issues with utime and machine.RTC, and the error signatures in the issue (1994 dates instead of current dates) match the root cause that PR #5972 fixes.

Action: Close issue #16490 as resolved by PR #5972. The PR explicitly fixes the epoch issue causing incorrect mktime() results on ESP32.

#8728 ESP32 and PPP not workingIssue #7021 ESP32: network.PPP on UART not working, interrupt-issue?closed1.475DUPLICATE
Both issues describe the exact same root problem: ESP32 PPP ...

Both issues describe the exact same root problem: ESP32 PPP connections failing because UART data is not being processed immediately by the PPP object. Issue #8728 provides detailed analysis showing PPP ConfRequest acknowledgments are delayed ~6 seconds, while issue #7021 observes data accumulating in the UART rx buffer without PPP processing it. Both users suspect interrupt-driven UART issues on ESP32. The symptoms (PPP not responding to modem handshakes, data stuck in UART buffer, connection failure) are identical. Issue #8728 is more detailed but both address the same underlying bug in the ESP32 UART/PPP integration.

Action: Close issue #7021 as duplicate of #8728. The newer issue (#8728) has substantially better diagnostic detail including specific timing analysis (6-second delays), FSM state observations, and log evidence. Keep #8728 open for investigation and resolution.

#10751 codeformat.py bugIssue #18953 tools/codeformat.py: Fix IndexError and improve robustness in fixup_cclosed1.456DUPLICATE
Issue #10751 reports a bug in codeformat.py where the fixup_...

Issue #10751 reports a bug in codeformat.py where the fixup_c function crashes with 'IndexError: list index out of range' at line 136 when accessing dedent_stack[-1]. Issue #18953 explicitly addresses this exact same bug, providing a fix for the IndexError caused by incorrect array indexing when preprocessor directives appear near the end of files, as well as adding protection against dedent_stack underflow. The candidate issue describes the same root cause (empty dedent_stack access) and provides the solution code that resolves the problem reported in the query.

Action: Close issue #10751 as resolved by issue #18953. The candidate issue contains the fix for the reported bug.

#5046 RFC: MICROPY_PY_BUILTINS_NEXT2 always set to (0) ?PR #4400 RFC py: Support builtin next() with 2 argumentsclosed1.451DUPLICATE
Issue #5046 reports that MICROPY_PY_BUILTINS_NEXT2 is always...

Issue #5046 reports that MICROPY_PY_BUILTINS_NEXT2 is always set to 0 (except in coverage), breaking CPython compatibility for the next() built-in function. PR #4400 is an RFC that implements exactly this feature: it adds support for the 2-argument next() builtin, introduces the MICROPY_PY_BUILTINS_NEXT2 configuration option, and enables it in the coverage build. The PR directly addresses the root cause identified in the issue by implementing the missing functionality and providing the configuration mechanism.

Action: Close issue #5046 as resolved by PR #4400. The PR implements the requested feature and addresses the compatibility issue described in the RFC.

#6773 uncaught exception in Timer(1) interrupt handler MemoryError:Issue #6088 Unable to catch MemoryError in ISRclosed1.447DUPLICATE
Both issues describe the exact same underlying problem: Memo...

Both issues describe the exact same underlying problem: MemoryError exceptions raised within Timer interrupt callbacks cannot be caught by try/except blocks, resulting in 'uncaught exception in Timer(X) interrupt handler' errors. Issue #6773 shows a user experiencing this with I2C/OLED operations in a Timer callback, while #6088 provides a minimal reproducible example demonstrating that MemoryError in ISR callbacks bypasses exception handling. The error signatures are identical ('uncaught exception in Timer(...) interrupt handler MemoryError'), and both involve STM32 Timer interrupts. Issue #6088 is more precise in diagnosing the root cause (exception handling failure in ISR context), making it the canonical report that resolves #6773.

Action: Close issue #6773 as a duplicate of #6088. The core problem is that MemoryError exceptions in interrupt handlers cannot be caught, which is the subject of #6088. Users experiencing this should refer to #6088 for the detailed diagnosis and potential solutions.

#17357 esp32: `misc/rge_sm.py` test fails with hardware floating point enabled.PR #17731 py/obj: Improve REPR_C accuracy and include it in CI testsmerged1.402DUPLICATE
The query reports that `tests/misc/rge_sm.py` fails on ESP32...

The query reports that `tests/misc/rge_sm.py` fails on ESP32 with hardware floating point enabled (MICROPY_FLOAT_IMPL_FLOAT) due to float rounding bias. The candidate PR #17731 explicitly addresses this by fixing the REPR_C implementation to reduce rounding bias toward zero, and specifically modifies `tests/misc/rge_sm.py` to handle the corrected behavior. The PR description mentions `misc/rge_sm` as one of the test cases affected by the bias issue. This PR resolves the root cause of the test failure reported in the query.

Action: Close issue #17357 as resolved by PR #17731. The PR fixes the floating point rounding bias in REPR_C that caused the rge_sm test to fail, and updates the test to work with the corrected behavior.

#8208 RP2: Overclock or CPU_MHZ as a board config optionPR #8972 ports/rp2: Add board overclock config.open1.402DUPLICATE
The PR #8972 explicitly states 'This is a first shot at impl...

The PR #8972 explicitly states 'This is a first shot at implementing #8208' in its description. The PR implements the exact feature requested in the issue: a board configuration mechanism to set CPU frequency and voltage for RP2040 overclocking. The issue requested a way to configure CPU frequency and overvolt without maintaining forks, and the PR adds MICROPY_HW_CLOCK_KHZ and MICROPY_HW_VREG_VOLTAGE macros to achieve this. The PR directly resolves the feature request.

Action: Close issue #8208 as resolved by PR #8972

#13639 STM32: BLE Bonding Example does not workIssue #8264 example->bluetooth->ble_bonding_peripheral.py error with PYBD-SF2 and PYBD_SF6closed1.376DUPLICATE
Both issues report the exact same error (OSError: -513) when...

Both issues report the exact same error (OSError: -513) when running the ble_bonding_peripheral.py example on STM32-based PyBoard boards (PYBD-SF2, PYBD-SF6). The error occurs at the same point in the code (during BLE advertisement setup after IRK secret configuration). Issue #13639 provides the solution (uncommenting addr_mode configuration), while issue #8264 reports the same problem without the solution. These are clearly the same underlying bug affecting the same example on the same port.

Action: Close issue #8264 as a duplicate of #13639, since #13639 contains the complete problem description and the identified solution.

#4873 stm32: time.time() always returning 0Issue #2457 stmhal: RTC stops ticking and cannot be updated after soft rebootclosed1.373DUPLICATE
Both issues describe the exact same root cause: the STM32 RT...

Both issues describe the exact same root cause: the STM32 RTC peripheral fails to resume operation after a soft reset (ctrl-D/machine.reset). Issue #4873 reports time.time() returning 0 after soft reset on PYBD_SF6W, while #2457 reports the RTC stopping and datetime() setter hanging after soft reboot on STM32F439/STM32F411. The symptoms are identical (RTC frozen after soft reset, only power cycle fixes it), affecting the same component (stm32/rtc.c), and the underlying cause is the RTC not being properly reinitialized during soft reset. This is a known STM32 RTC issue that was addressed in later MicroPython versions.

Action: Close issue #4873 as a duplicate of #2457. Both issues describe the same RTC soft-reset bug on STM32 ports. The fix for this issue was implemented in MicroPython v1.11+ where the RTC is properly reinitialized during soft reset.

#13599 The BLE.Config gives `ValueError: unknown config param` with pairing parametersPR #6662 extmod/modbluetooth: Add pairing/bonding support (v2)merged1.366DUPLICATE
Issue #13599 reports that BLE.config() raises 'ValueError: u...

Issue #13599 reports that BLE.config() raises 'ValueError: unknown config param' when trying to set pairing parameters on PICO-W. The candidate PR #6662 explicitly adds pairing/bonding support to the Bluetooth module, including the implementation of pairing configuration parameters. The PR modifies extmod/modbluetooth.c and adds the example file ble_bonding_peripheral.py that the issue reporter was trying to use. The error in the issue occurs because the pairing configuration parameters were not yet implemented in the version the user was running (v1.22.1 from Jan 2024). PR #6662 implements this functionality, resolving the root cause of the issue.

Action: Close issue #13599 as resolved by PR #6662. The pairing/bonding support was added in this PR, which includes the necessary configuration parameters that were missing when the issue was reported.

#3462 Add VM hook after every opcode (MICROPY_VM_HOOK_OPCODE)PR #1849 py/vm: Add macros to hook into various points in the VM.closed1.364DUPLICATE
The query (Issue #3462) requests a VM hook mechanism to allo...

The query (Issue #3462) requests a VM hook mechanism to allow user-defined callbacks (like RTOS.yield()) to be inserted at controlled intervals during opcode dispatch. The candidate (PR #1849) implements exactly this functionality by adding configurable hook macros (MICROPY_VM_HOOK_INIT, MICROPY_VM_HOOK_LOOP, MICROPY_VM_HOOK_RETURN) to the VM. The PR description explicitly mentions the common use case of inserting polling checks after a certain number of opcodes, which directly addresses the user's need for cooperative multitasking integration. The implementation provides the extensibility the user requested, allowing ports to define their own hook behavior in mpconfigport.h.

Action: Close issue #3462 as resolved by PR #1849. The feature request has been fully implemented with the addition of MICROPY_VM_HOOK_* macros that allow ports to insert custom logic at VM execution points.

PR #13085 directly fixes the exact problem described in issue #7331: CMake clean leaving stale makeqstrdefs.py hash files that break subsequent builds. The PR modifies both py/mkrules.cmake (adding BYPRODUCTS directives so cmake tracks the hash files) and py/makeqstrdefs.py (making the script robust to missing output files). This addresses the root cause and the specific fix suggested in the issue.

Action: Close issue #7331 as resolved by PR #13085.

#2720 Can't get property value of the superclassIssue #18741 super() does not support propertiesclosed1.287DUPLICATE
Both issues describe the exact same bug: calling super().pro...

Both issues describe the exact same bug: calling super().property_name in a subclass returns the property object itself instead of invoking the parent's getter. Issue #2720 shows the property object being returned and failing when trying to use it as a dict. Issue #18741 shows the same issue with string concatenation failing because the property object is returned instead of the string value. Both have identical root causes in the descriptor protocol implementation for super() and property access. The candidate issue (#18741) is a clearer, more minimal reproduction of the same underlying problem described in #2720.

Action: Close issue #2720 as a duplicate of #18741, as #18741 provides a cleaner reproduction case of the same bug.

#7998 stm32: Support pins with multiple AFs for the same peripheral.Issue #6814 SPI2 on STM32WB55 pin erroropen1.257DUPLICATE
Both issues describe the exact same root problem: on STM32WB...

Both issues describe the exact same root problem: on STM32WB55, pin PD3 cannot be correctly configured as SPI2_MISO because the pin configuration system assumes each peripheral uses a single AF number, but PD3 has SPI2_SCK on AF3 and SPI2_MISO on AF5. Issue #7998 provides the comprehensive technical analysis of the `pin_find_af` and `AF_FN_SPI` enum limitation, while issue #6814 reports the specific symptom (PD3 switching to SPI2_SCK instead of SPI2_MISO) and proposes a similar fix approach. The affected code, board (STM32WB55), and specific pin/peripheral conflict are identical.

Action: Close issue #6814 as a duplicate of #7998, as #7998 provides the complete technical analysis and solution path for the same underlying problem.

Action: Close issue #16490 as resolved by PR #5973. The PR explicitly fixes the epoch issue causing incorrect Unix timestamp calculations on ESP32.

#11082 RP2 SPI: Instantiating with spi=None does not behave sensibly.PR #16428 rp2/spi: Allow omitting of unused pinsclosed1.072DUPLICATE
The issue #11082 reports that the RP2 SPI constructor accept...

The issue #11082 reports that the RP2 SPI constructor accepts `None` for MISO/MOSI pins but still instantiates default pins, causing pin conflicts. The user requests that `None` should either be rejected or (preferably) not instantiate a pin. PR #16428 directly addresses this by changing the behavior so that when a pin is set as `None`, it is omitted rather than using the default pin. The PR description explicitly states 'The change makes it possible to omit unused SPI pins. This can be achieved by setting a pin as `None`' and notes that 'Previously if a pin was set as `None` the default pin number for this interface has been used.' This is exactly the fix requested in the issue.

Action: Close issue #11082 as resolved by PR #16428. The PR implements the requested feature to allow omitting unused SPI pins by passing None, which prevents the pin conflicts described in the issue.

#4290 uselect: poll instance produces unexpected results under fault conditionsIssue #3426 uasyncio.await reader.read()(in progress) remains indefinitely pendingclosed1.060DUPLICATE
Both issues describe the exact same underlying problem: uasy...

Both issues describe the exact same underlying problem: uasyncio StreamReader/StreamWriter objects hang indefinitely when a socket closes or encounters a fault condition because uselect.poll fails to detect POLLERR/POLLHUP events. Issue #4290 provides comprehensive testing across platforms (Unix, ESP8266, ESP32) showing poll doesn't trigger error events on socket faults. Issue #3426 reports the same symptom with a closed socket (state -16) causing reader.read() to hang indefinitely. Both explicitly mention the need for uselect.poll to detect closed sockets. The root cause and expected behavior are identical.

Action: Close issue #3426 as a duplicate of #4290. Issue #4290 contains more detailed testing data and platform-specific analysis, making it the better reference for tracking this bug.

#6618 ESP32: dhcp_hostname is read-only on non-WLAN interfacesIssue #12817 Unable to set hostname on esp32 for LAN DHCP requestclosed1.026DUPLICATE
Both issues describe the exact same problem: the inability t...

Both issues describe the exact same problem: the inability to set a custom DHCP hostname on ESP32 LAN (Ethernet) interfaces. Issue #6618 reports that `lan.config(dhcp_hostname='...')` raises 'ValueError: unknown config param', while issue #12817 reports that even when attempting various configuration methods, the DHCP request always uses the hardcoded 'espressif' hostname. Both are feature requests/bug reports for enabling hostname configuration on ESP32 wired network interfaces. The underlying root cause is the same: the LAN interface implementation does not support the dhcp_hostname configuration parameter.

Action: Close issue #12817 as a duplicate of #6618 (or vice versa, depending on which is older). The fix should implement support for the dhcp_hostname config parameter in the ESP32 LAN interface, similar to how it works for WLAN interfaces.

The PR changes py/mpconfig.h to enable the feature at the basic feature level and removes the cpydiff test that documented the incompatibility. The PR resolves the underlying request of #5046 regardless of which issue number it cites.

The candidate PR #6080 explicitly addresses these exact issues by implementing 'full uos.dupterm support' for the Unix port, including proper handle returns, complete stdio management, and fixing the blocking behavior. The PR description mentions resolving the limitations of the current implementation and enabling proper stream handling. The affected code components (ports/unix/main.c, extmod/os_dupterm.c, extmod/vfs_posix_file.c) match the error signatures in the query. This PR directly resolves the reported bug.

Action: Close issue #18555 as resolved by PR #6080. The PR implements the full dupterm support that fixes the blocking and routing issues described in the bug report.

The PR explicitly states 'Also fixes #18982' in its description. The PR description confirms testing on ESP32-P4 board showing the fix works. This is a direct resolution of the reported bug.

It also adds a regression test in pyb_can2.py covering the exact scenario from the issue report.

The PR adds the capability to fall back to LSI when LSE times out, which was the core feature request in the issue. The PR also addresses related concerns like initialization timing and fault tolerance mentioned in the issue.

Action: Close issue #1546 as resolved by PR #1599. The feature has been implemented with LSI fallback support when LSE initialization fails or times out.

Action: Close issue #3218 as a duplicate of #2093, noting that it is a follow-up discussion regarding the same RFC. If #2093 is still open, keep it as the primary tracking issue for the sensor driver interface RFC.

Issue #682 is an older issue that explicitly lists 'Support signed param' as a TODO item for these same methods. Both issues affect the same code components (py/objint.c) and describe the same underlying problem: incomplete implementation of int.to_bytes() and int.from_bytes(). Issue #682 covers the exact functionality that #15399 is reporting as missing.

Action: Close issue #15399 as a duplicate of issue #682, since #682 already tracks the incomplete implementation of the signed parameter for these methods.

Issue #11450 reports the bug (no mDNS packets observed), while #11545 confirms the issue and proposes documentation/fix actions. The candidate (#11545) directly addresses the query's problem by confirming the bug and discussing resolution strategies.

Action: Close issue #11545 as a duplicate of #11450, as it is a follow-up discussion confirming the same underlying bug reported in #11450.

#731 Raspberry Pi Pico and SD Card moduleIssue #656 Raspberry Pi Pico SD Card Errorsopen0.906DUPLICATE
Both issues report the same core problem: SD card I/O errors...

Both issues report the same core problem: SD card I/O errors (specifically OSError: [Errno 5] EIO) on the Raspberry Pi Pico when using the sdcard.py module. Issue #731 shows the error occurring during readblocks at line 231, while issue #656 shows the same error occurring during writeblocks at line 279, along with timeout errors. Both involve SPI communication with SD cards on the RP2 port, and both result in filesystem corruption or I/O failures. The underlying root cause is the same - SD card initialization/communication issues on the Raspberry Pi Pico port.

Action: Close issue #731 as a duplicate of issue #656, as #656 provides more comprehensive details about the problem including multiple error types, code examples, and the pattern of filesystem corruption. The maintainer should investigate the SD card SPI implementation for the RP2 port.

The PR modifies the relevant ESP8266 UART files (ports/esp8266/machine_uart.c, ports/esp8266/uart.c, ports/esp8266/uart.h) and updates documentation. This PR directly resolves the feature request in the issue.

Action: Close issue #4389 as resolved by PR #9116. The requested uart.flush() functionality has been implemented and merged.

#18658 mpremote cp fails with equals sign (=) in filenamePR #18670 Fix multiple unicode issues in mpremote.closed0.780DUPLICATE
The candidate PR #18670 explicitly references and fixes issu...

The candidate PR #18670 explicitly references and fixes issue #18658 in its description ('Fixes: #18658'). The PR addresses the exact problem described in the query: the mpremote argument parser incorrectly interpreting '=' in filenames as key-value separators. The PR summary mentions 'CLI parsing fix: Command expansion no longer misinterprets arguments containing =, preventing unexpected-argument errors' which directly resolves the issue reported in #18658.

Action: Close issue #18658 as resolved by PR #18670

#1555 stmhal: uart chars are being dropped, even when only at 115200 baudPR #1644 stmhal: Increase the priority of UART IRQ.closed0.776DUPLICATE
The issue #1555 reports UART characters being dropped on STM...

The issue #1555 reports UART characters being dropped on STM32 due to interrupts being disabled or preempted for 694-847 microseconds, which far exceeds the character time at 115200 baud (86.8 µs). The PR #1644 directly addresses this root cause by increasing the UART interrupt priority from 13 to 1, ensuring UART IRQs are not preempted by lower-priority interrupts. The PR description explicitly states 'The UARTs have no FIFOs, so if interrupts are disabled for more than a character time... then characters get dropped' - which is exactly the problem described in the issue. The affected code components (stmhal/uart, IRQ handling) match perfectly.

Action: Close issue #1555 as resolved by PR #1644. The PR implements the fix needed to prevent UART character dropping by increasing interrupt priority.

#14268 [Aioble] Update Parameters request from Central not answered by Peripheral.Issue #841 [Aioble] Update Parameters request from Central not answered by Peripheral.closed0.770DUPLICATE
Both items describe the exact same issue: MicroPython Aioble...

Both items describe the exact same issue: MicroPython Aioble peripheral failing to respond to LE Connection Parameter Update requests from Windows 11's Bleak client, causing connection freezes. The QUERY item (issue #14268 in micropython/micropython) and CANDIDATE item (issue #841 in micropython/micropython-lib) have identical titles, reference the same upstream bleak issue (#1262), cite the same Wireshark analysis, and describe the same symptoms (Windows 11 sends LE Connection Update while Windows 10 doesn't, causing the peripheral to freeze). The CANDIDATE provides additional traceback details but the core problem is identical.

Action: Close issue #841 in micropython/micropython-lib as a duplicate of issue #14268 in micropython/micropython, since the root cause lies in the core MicroPython Bluetooth stack (extmod/modbluetooth.c, rp2/bluetooth.c) rather than the library. The fix should be implemented in the main repository.

The issue #18922 reports that instantiating CAN2 corrupts CAN1's filter settings on STM32, and the PR fixes this exact problem by addressing the CAN1/CAN2 filter corruption issue in the Classic CAN implementation. The PR includes test coverage for this specific scenario and modifies the same code areas (ports/stm32/can.c and ports/stm32/pyb_can.c) mentioned in the issue.

Action: Close issue #18922 as resolved by PR #18992

This PR directly resolves the root cause of the issue by implementing the internal flash storage support for VFS that was missing.

#6997 Esp8266 doesn't execute code after woke up from deep sleepIssue #3961 ESP8266 crashes on wake from deepsleepclosed0.752DUPLICATE
Both issues describe the exact same underlying problem: ESP8...

Both issues describe the exact same underlying problem: ESP8266 failing to execute code after waking from deep sleep triggered by an RTC alarm. Issue #3961 (older) reports a crash/unresponsive state after wake, while issue #6997 (newer) reports code halting silently after wake. Both involve the same components (esp8266/rtc, machine.RTC, machine.deepsleep) and the same root cause (RTC alarm wake-up sequence bug in ESP8266 port). The symptoms differ slightly (crash vs silent halt) but represent the same firmware bug. Issue #3961 provides more diagnostic detail and is the original report.

#652 traceback.format_exc failed due to sys.exc_info not existIssue #5110 Availability of sys.exc_info() in current buildsclosed0.738DUPLICATE
Both issues report the exact same underlying problem: `sys.e...

Both issues report the exact same underlying problem: `sys.exc_info()` is not implemented in MicroPython. Issue #652 shows the symptom when `traceback.format_exc()` fails due to missing `sys.exc_info()`, while Issue #5110 directly reports that `sys.exc_info()` is missing. Both produce the same error signature 'AttributeError: module object has no attribute exc_info'. The root cause is identical - the absence of `sys.exc_info()` implementation in the sys module. This is a feature request/bug report for the same missing functionality.

Action: Close issue #652 as a duplicate of #5110 (or vice versa, depending on which is older/more detailed). Both issues describe the same missing feature. If a PR exists that implements `sys.exc_info()`, close both issues referencing that PR.

#12048 STM32H5 machine.ADC core temperature sensor not workingPR #12152 stm32/machine_adc: Add STM32H5 support.merged0.734DUPLICATE
PR #12152 directly implements the exact fix described in iss...

PR #12152 directly implements the exact fix described in issue #12048. The issue identifies that adc_config_channel() fails to decode encoded channel numbers (0xc3210000) via __LL_ADC_CHANNEL_TO_DECIMAL_NB() before configuring the SMPR register, and explicitly suggests this as the fix. PR #12152 does precisely this — it introduces machine_adc_internal_ch_t enum to replace incompatible STM32 literal constants and calls __LL_ADC_CHANNEL_TO_DECIMAL_NB() in adc_config_channel(). The PR's test output confirms CORE_TEMP now reads 31.57°C (was -199°C), resolving the reported symptom. The PR also credits @yn386 for discussions, suggesting direct awareness of this report.

Action: Close issue #12048 as resolved by PR #12152, once (or if) that PR is merged.

#8923 Pi Pico W AP mode unable to set subnetIssue #17977 Cannot change default exposed IP (for DHCP etc) on Pico W running in AP mode (remains exposing default 192.168.4.x)open0.732DUPLICATE
Both issues describe the exact same bug: on the Pi Pico W (R...

Both issues describe the exact same bug: on the Pi Pico W (RP2 port), when configuring a WLAN in AP mode with a custom IP/subnet via ifconfig(), the DHCP server continues to use the default 192.168.4.x range instead of the configured values. Issue #8923 reports the subnet mismatch (configured 192.168.30.0/24 but clients get 192.168.4.x), while issue #17977 reports the gateway/DHCP range remaining at 192.168.4.1 despite configuration. Both affect the same components (rp2/network, extmod/modnetwork) and have identical symptoms and root cause.

Action: Close issue #17977 as a duplicate of #8923 (or vice versa, depending on which has more detail). Since #8923 appears to be the original report with clearer visual evidence, close #17977 as duplicate of #8923.

#3511 ujson dumps NaN but doesn't load itPR #5138 extmod/ujson: Add support for CPython-compatible nan/inf handling.open0.719DUPLICATE
The PR #5138 explicitly states 'This is a fix for #3511' in ...

The PR #5138 explicitly states 'This is a fix for #3511' in its description. The issue #3511 reports that ujson.dumps() outputs 'nan' but ujson.loads() fails to parse it back. The PR adds support for parsing NaN/Infinity/-Infinity values in the JSON loading code, directly resolving the round-trip serialization problem described in the issue. The PR also introduces the allow_nan keyword argument for dump() and dumps() to control whether these representations are created, matching CPython's implementation.

Action: Close issue #3511 as resolved by PR #5138

#18025 socket.bind for AF_UNIX requires raw sockaddr structIssue #2464 Binding AF_UNIX sockets failsclosed0.714DUPLICATE
Both issues describe the exact same underlying problem: AF_U...

Both issues describe the exact same underlying problem: AF_UNIX socket.bind() in MicroPython's Unix port fails when passed a string path, requiring a raw sockaddr struct instead. Issue #2464 (older, v1.8.4) reports the issue with OSError [Errno 22] EINVAL, and issue #18025 (newer, v1.26.0) reports the same problem with the same error. Both involve the same components (socket module, unix port) and the same root cause - the bind() function doesn't accept string paths for AF_UNIX sockets like CPython does. This is a clear case of the same bug reported across different MicroPython versions.

Action: Close issue #18025 as a duplicate of issue #2464, since #2464 is the older issue that first reported this problem. The newer issue (#18025) has a more detailed reproduction and version info, but the core problem is identical. If a fix has been implemented since #2464, check if it resolves both issues.

#4207 [NUCLEO_H743ZI] changing CPU frequency does not work as expectedIssue #4737 ports/stm32: Can't change CPU frequency on NUCLEO_F401RE boardclosed0.683DUPLICATE
Both issues report the exact same error message 'can't chang...

Both issues report the exact same error message 'can't change freq' when attempting to change CPU frequency via machine.freq() on STM32 boards. Issue #4207 describes the problem on NUCLEO_H743ZI (STM32H7) where frequency changes cause UART/USB issues, while #4737 reports the same failure on NUCLEO_F401RE (STM32F4). The underlying root cause is identical: the machine.freq() implementation in ports/stm32/machine_freq.c fails to properly handle frequency changes, causing system instability or fatal errors. Both issues affect the same code component and represent the same bug across different STM32 variants.

Action: Close issue #4737 as a duplicate of #4207 (or vice versa depending on which has more detail). The root cause is the same machine.freq() implementation issue in the STM32 port. A fix to the frequency scaling code in ports/stm32/machine_freq.c would resolve both issues.

#5911 extmod/uasyncio: Exception handling differences to CPythonPR #6664 extmod/uasyncio: Delay calling Loop.call_exception_handler by 1 loop, and add Task.done() methodmerged0.666DUPLICATE
The issue #5911 reports incompatibilities in exception handl...

The issue #5911 reports incompatibilities in exception handling between MicroPython's uasyncio and CPython's asyncio, specifically that uncaught task exceptions are delivered immediately in MicroPython rather than being deferred until the task is garbage collected or cancelled. The PR #6664 explicitly addresses this by changing the behavior to delay calling Loop.call_exception_handler by one loop iteration, allowing other tasks a chance to await and catch the exception. The PR description directly references the problem described in the issue and implements the fix to make MicroPython's behavior more compatible with CPython.

Action: Close issue #5911 as resolved by PR #6664. The PR implements the exact fix needed to address the exception handling differences reported in the issue.

Both issues describe the exact same problem: on ESP32, enabl...

Both issues describe the exact same problem: on ESP32, enabling WiFi AP mode prevents newly created threads from running. Issue #7074 provides detailed context with a boot.py example showing STA+AP mode causing thread execution failure, while #7368 is a concise version of the same issue. The affected components (esp32/network, thread) and symptoms are identical. This is a clear case of duplicate reporting of the same underlying bug.

Action: Close issue #7368 as a duplicate of #7074 (the more detailed report with reproduction code). Keep #7074 open for investigation and resolution.

The candidate (PR #11816) explicitly adds Python linkage for SO_BROADCAST to enable UDP broadcast functionality. The PR modifies the exact same components (extmod/modsocket.c, extmod/modlwip.c) and addresses the exact same missing constant. This is a direct resolution of the reported bug.

#16912 When miso=None is set, the default miso pin changed state.PR #16922 ports/rp2: Optional spi_id and optional disabling of MISO pinclosed0.653DUPLICATE
The PR explicitly references issue #16912 in its description...

The PR explicitly references issue #16912 in its description ('See: https://github.com/micropython/micropython/issues/16912') and directly addresses the reported bug. The issue describes that setting miso=None causes the default MISO pin (pin 16) to become unavailable for GPIO operations. The PR implements exactly the fix needed: making machine.SPI(miso=None) explicitly disable the MISO pin so it doesn't claim the default pin, allowing it to be used for other purposes like register select pins for LCDs.

Action: Close issue #16912 as resolved by PR #16922

PR #1192 implements exactly this capability by adding a 'getlbl' pseudo-instruction that returns the pc/offset pair needed to address data created with data() statements. The PR description explicitly states 'This allows data created with the data() statement to be accessed using labels' and references the test file 'tests/asmdatalabels.py' which would test the functionality requested in the issue. The PR directly resolves the problem described in the issue.

Action: Close issue #1142 as resolved by PR #1192

Both occur during file close operations after writes. Issue #6568 is older and describes the same underlying bug in the littlefs2 implementation on ESP32.

Both issues describe the exact same bug: calling i2c.scan() ...

Both issues describe the exact same bug: calling i2c.scan() on ESP8266 causes a watchdog reset (rst cause:2) with identical boot ROM output patterns. Issue #8876 explicitly references issue #1996 and describes the same crash symptoms (garbled text, boot mode changes) as #9117. Both affect the same code path (esp8266/machine_i2c.c) and occur with the same firmware versions (v1.19.1+). The only differences are minor variations in pin configuration and boot mode numbers, which are expected for different ESP8266 boards.

Action: Close issue #8876 as a duplicate of #9117 (or vice versa, depending on which has more detail). Both describe the same underlying I2C scan crash bug on ESP8266.

#969 MQTT subscribe Remaining Length encoding is incorrectIssue #284 umqtt subscribe to topic with 23 bytes failsopen0.584DUPLICATE
Both issues describe the exact same bug in the umqtt.simple ...

Both issues describe the exact same bug in the umqtt.simple library's subscribe method. Issue #969 identifies the root cause: the Remaining Length field is encoded as a single byte instead of a Variable Byte Integer per MQTT spec V3.1.1, limiting topic length to 127 bytes. Issue #284 reports the practical symptom: subscribe fails with OSError when topic exceeds 122 bytes (the actual limit due to other packet overhead). The candidate issue even references the same underlying problem and mentions the 127-byte boundary. Both point to the same code location and same fix is needed.

Action: Close issue #284 as duplicate of #969 (or vice versa depending on which is older). The root cause analysis in #969 is more complete, so if #969 is older, close #284. If #284 is older, close #969 and keep #284 as it has better reproduction steps. A merged PR that fixes the Variable Byte Integer encoding in the subscribe method would resolve both issues.

#18868 tests: cmdline/repl_lock.py and repl_cont.py intermittent failuresPR #18861 tests/run-tests.py: Add automatic retry for known-flaky tests.merged0.562DUPLICATE
The issue explicitly states "PR #18861 now ignores thes...

The issue explicitly states "PR #18861 now ignores these failures in CI" and was filed to document the problem that PR #18861 addresses. The PR adds both `repl_lock.py` and `repl_cont.py` to its flaky-test allowlist with retry logic, directly resolving the CI instability described in the issue. The PR is the fix; the issue is the problem report.

Action: Close issue #18868 as resolved by PR #18861 (or once it merges). The issue documents the problem; the PR implements the mitigation.

#8965 docs: Can recv example needs updated to reflect 5 return values in the lst in V1.19.1PR #9311 docs/library/pyb.CAN.rst: Update the recv examplemerged0.561DUPLICATE
PR #9311 directly fixes the exact issue reported in #8965: u...

PR #9311 directly fixes the exact issue reported in #8965: updating the CAN.recv() documentation example to reflect 5 return values instead of 4. The PR modifies docs/library/pyb.CAN.rst, correcting the list structure from 4 to 5 elements to match the actual method signature introduced in v1.19.1.

Action: Close issue #8965 as resolved by PR #9311.

#400 OSError: -40Issue #473 Get request fails with OSerror: -40closed0.543DUPLICATE
Both issues report the exact same error (OSError: -40) when ...

Both issues report the exact same error (OSError: -40) when making HTTPS requests via urequests on ESP8266. The query (issue #400) and candidate (issue #473) both describe failures with ussl.wrap_socket() when connecting to HTTPS servers, while HTTP requests work fine. The synopsis of issue #400 explicitly identifies the root cause as axtls SSL library incompatibility with modern TLS cipher suites, which explains why issue #473's HTTPS request fails. Both issues affect the same components (esp8266/network, extmod/ussl) and have identical error signatures. This is a clear case of the same underlying problem reported in two different issues.

Action: Close issue #473 as a duplicate of issue #400, as issue #400 contains the more detailed analysis and root cause identification. The fix for this issue requires either using a server with compatible cipher suites or finding an alternative SSL implementation for ESP8266.

#11882 ESP32: target_copile options are not populated to moduleIssue #18880 cmake: Route usermod sources through native port build systems.open0.540DUPLICATE
Issue #11882 reports that target_compile_options passed to t...

Issue #11882 reports that target_compile_options passed to the usermod interface target are not being propagated to .cc source files in the ESP32 build system. Issue #18880 explicitly identifies this as the root cause: the INTERFACE library pattern used for usermod sources doesn't propagate compilation flags from platform-specific build systems (like ESP-IDF's add_compile_options during idf_component_register). The candidate issue provides a detailed analysis of the problem and proposes a fix to route usermod sources through native port build systems instead of using the INTERFACE library indirection. This directly resolves the issue reported in #11882.

Action: Close issue #11882 as resolved by issue #18880. The candidate issue provides both the root cause analysis and the architectural fix needed to resolve the compile_options propagation problem.

#1375 unix usocket: External representation of (binary) addressesPR #1002 Make unix usocket connect and bind functions more similar to CPythonclosed0.518DUPLICATE
The issue #1375 explicitly references PR #1002 in its descri...

The issue #1375 explicitly references PR #1002 in its description, stating it was closed because the approach of requiring getaddrinfo() to pass addresses works. The issue then discusses the opposite problem (how to represent addresses received from socket functions like accept()). PR #1002 addresses the original problem mentioned in the issue (making connect/bind accept tuple arguments), and the issue is essentially a follow-up discussion about the complementary problem. The issue author acknowledges PR #1002's approach as the solution to the first part of the problem, making this a direct continuation of the same discussion thread.

Action: Close issue #1375 as duplicate of PR #1002, noting that the PR addresses the primary concern and the issue represents a follow-up discussion about the complementary problem that was acknowledged in the PR's context.

#5322 Listing class attributes in the defined orderPR #6130 py/modbuiltins: Keep class locals ordered as per their definition.open0.513DUPLICATE
The PR explicitly references the issue in its description ('...

The PR explicitly references the issue in its description ('As discussed in https://github.com/micropython/micropython/issues/5322#issuecomment-641035846'). The PR implements the requested feature: preserving class attribute definition order. The issue asked for a way to list class attributes in declaration order, and this PR adds the MICROPY_PY_CLASS_ORDERED_LOCALS build flag to achieve exactly that. The test file 'tests/basics/class_dict_order.py' confirms this addresses the core problem.

Action: Close issue #5322 as resolved by PR #6130. The feature has been implemented and tested.

#2789 Interactive REPL does not support unicode/utf8Issue #14255 [shared] [webassembly] pyexec_event_repl_process_char unable to understand unicodeopen0.484DUPLICATE
Both issues describe the exact same underlying problem: the ...

Both issues describe the exact same underlying problem: the MicroPython REPL fails to correctly handle Unicode/UTF-8 characters when typed directly at the prompt. Issue #2789 reports that Unicode characters disappear in REPL mode (though paste mode works), while issue #14255 provides more detailed examples (µpython becoming 'python', emoji sequences becoming empty) and identifies the root cause in pyexec_event_repl_process_char/replProcessChar. Both note that paste mode and input() work correctly. The candidate issue (#14255) is a more detailed report of the same bug described in the query (#2789).

Action: Close issue #2789 as a duplicate of #14255, as #14255 contains more detailed reproduction steps, specific examples, and identifies the affected code component (pyexec_event_repl_process_char).

#2762 os.utime is missingPR #9644 moduos: Add os.utime() support to vfs layer and LFS2, FAT and posix filesystems.open0.461DUPLICATE
The query (Issue #2762) requests the implementation of `os.u...

The query (Issue #2762) requests the implementation of `os.utime()` to update file timestamps on MicroPython devices. The candidate (PR #9644) explicitly implements this feature, adding `os.utime()` support to the VFS layer and multiple filesystems (LFS2, FAT, POSIX). The PR description even mentions the exact use case described in the issue: 'preserving timestamps of files and directory structures copied onto (or synchronised) between micropython boards and host systems'. The PR includes comprehensive tests for the new functionality.

Action: Close issue #2762 as resolved by PR #9644. The feature request has been fully implemented.

#2028 super() appears not to work with multiple inheritanceIssue #6077 Multiple inheritance not working but also not throwing any erroropen0.433DUPLICATE
Both issues report the same fundamental problem: MicroPython...

Both issues report the same fundamental problem: MicroPython's multiple inheritance implementation does not correctly follow CPython's Method Resolution Order (MRO). Issue #2028 shows super() skipping intermediate classes (D→B→A, missing C), while #6077 shows method lookup failing to find methods from all base classes (Test1 missing 'two', Test2 missing 'one'). Both describe the same underlying bug in the MRO implementation affecting multiple inheritance scenarios. The symptoms differ slightly (super() vs direct method calls) but stem from the same root cause in how MicroPython traverses the inheritance chain.

Action: Close issue #6077 as a duplicate of #2028 (or vice versa depending on which is older/more detailed). Both issues describe the same MRO bug in multiple inheritance. If a fix has been implemented in a merged PR, reference that PR when closing.

#5123 bytes.find() differs from CPythonPR #11631 py/objstr.c: Add support for bytes.find(int).open0.432DUPLICATE
The query (issue #5123) reports that bytes.find() in MicroPy...

The query (issue #5123) reports that bytes.find() in MicroPython rejects integer arguments (0-255) that CPython accepts, raising a TypeError. The candidate (PR #11631) explicitly implements support for bytes.find(int) and bytes.index(int) to improve CPython compatibility. The PR description states 'As requested in #11617' and the synopsis confirms it adds support for integer arguments to bytes.find() and bytes.index(). This directly resolves the problem described in the query.

Action: Close issue #5123 as resolved by PR #11631. The PR implements the requested feature to allow bytes.find() to accept integer arguments (0-255) matching CPython behavior.

#7517 Issues with nrf52 portPR #15158 ports/nrf: Consolidate stdio functions.merged0.424DUPLICATE
Issue #7517 reports linker errors caused by multiple definit...

Issue #7517 reports linker errors caused by multiple definitions of stdio functions (mp_hal_stdin_rx_chr, mp_hal_stdout_tx_strn, etc.) when enabling both Bluetooth LE REPL and USB CDC simultaneously on the nRF52 port. PR #15158 explicitly addresses this by consolidating CDC, UART, and NUS stdio interfaces into a single unified handler, which resolves the symbol conflicts and allows these interfaces to be enabled separately. The PR description confirms it fixes the exact issue described in the query.

Action: Close issue #7517 as resolved by PR #15158. The PR directly fixes the linker errors and implements the requested capability to have Bluetooth and USB CDC REPL working simultaneously.

#18659 mpremote cat hangs on Windows interactive console with Unicode contentPR #18853 Fix multiple unicode issues in mpremote.open0.409DUPLICATE
The PR #18853 explicitly addresses the exact issue described...

The PR #18853 explicitly addresses the exact issue described in issue #18659. The PR title 'Fix multiple unicode issues in mpremote' and the description specifically mention 'resolves Unicode encoding errors on Windows consoles' and 'Unicode-safe Windows console output'. The issue #18659 describes mpremote cat hanging on Windows with Unicode content when stdout is interactive, and the PR fixes this by detecting modern consoles, setting UTF-8 code pages, and using raw UTF-8 writes. The affected code components match (tools/mpremote/console.py, transport.py, main.py). The PR directly resolves the hanging behavior described in the issue.

Action: Close issue #18659 as resolved by PR #18853

#18076 RP2: ADC pins does not work properly in XIAO RP2350PR #18440 Update the submodule pico-sdk to version 2.2.0 and make it compatibleclosed0.399DUPLICATE
The issue #18076 explicitly identifies that the ADC problem ...

The issue #18076 explicitly identifies that the ADC problem on XIAO RP2350 is caused by an outdated pico-sdk that lacks the ADC_BASE_PIN and NUM_ADC_CHANNELS macros. The PR #18440 directly addresses this by updating the pico-sdk submodule to v2.2.0, which includes the necessary changes. The PR description specifically mentions 'RP2350-based devices (including Seeed XIAO RP2350) with critical hardware macro definitions' and 'enhanced ADC/DAC functionality'. This is a direct resolution of the reported bug.

Action: Close issue #18076 as resolved by PR #18440. The PR explicitly updates the pico-sdk to the version that contains the required ADC macro definitions, which is the root cause fix identified in the issue.

Both issues describe the exact same bug: the uasyncio event loop exits prematurely when only tasks awaiting an Event are scheduled. The candidate provides a clear reproduction case showing the Event.wait() returning immediately instead of blocking when it's the sole task, which matches the behavior described in issue 5843.

Issue #979 reports that logging.basicConfig() fails to initi...

Issue #979 reports that logging.basicConfig() fails to initialize Handler and Formatter when called on an empty handler list because it checks Logger.handlers (list object) instead of Logger.hasHandlers() (boolean). Pull Request #806 explicitly addresses this by adding CPython-compatible logging improvements, including the handlers argument to basicConfig() and fixing the handler initialization logic. The PR description mentions 'avoids repeated handler addition' and 'uses a default formatter if a Handler has none set', which directly resolves the initialization failure described in #979. The PR also references fixing issues related to handler initialization and formatter defaults.

Action: Close issue #979 as resolved by PR #806. The PR implements the necessary fixes for basicConfig() initialization and handler/formatter setup that the issue reports as broken.

The PR explicitly states that using the legacy event loop AP...

The PR modifies the network module to use the new esp_netif and esp_event_loop_create_default APIs, which is exactly what's needed to enable mesh networking. This is a merged PR that fully resolves the feature request.

#11419 [ESP32 port] network_ppp does not manage pcb properly; can cause LWIP assertion or unmanaged pcbPR #17656 esp32/network_ppp: Bugfixes for deadlocks and crashes when disconnecting.merged0.376DUPLICATE
PR #17656 explicitly addresses the PCB cleanup bug described...

PR #17656 explicitly addresses the PCB cleanup bug described in issue #11419. The PR's third fix, "Correctly clean up PPP PCB after close," describes the identical root cause: pppapi_free fails when PPP is still connected, leaving the PCB allocated but unreferenced. The fix implements the correct behavior — triggering a disconnect and waiting for the lwIP callback to free the PCB — which directly resolves the assertion failures, memory leaks, and callback race conditions described in the issue. The PR also fixes the status callback deadlock, which relates to the ppp_status_cb() null-dereference scenario mentioned in the issue.

Action: Close issue #11419 as resolved by PR #17656. Verify PR #17656 has been merged before closing.

Both issues address the exact same problem: UART0 being disa...

Both issues address the exact same problem: UART0 being disabled on ESP32 because it's dedicated to the REPL. Issue #8218 asks why UART0 is blocked and what the implications are, while issue #3805 specifically requests the ability to detach UART0 from REPL using dupterm to support production hardware. The error signature 'UART(0) is disabled (dedicated to REPL)' appears in both. They describe the same underlying limitation and request the same solution (enabling UART0 usage). Issue #3805 provides a concrete use case (production hardware using RXD0/TXD0) that directly relates to the hardware design concerns mentioned in #8218.

Action: Close issue #8218 as a duplicate of #3805, as #3805 contains the specific feature request and use case that addresses the questions raised in #8218.

Since #245 explicitly addresses the implementation of the missing __del__ functionality that #1878 is requesting, they are duplicates.

#2628 Extend obj->iternext to handle not just __next__(), but also send() methodIssue #402 RFC: Implementing generic generator protocolclosed0.326DUPLICATE
Both issues address the same core problem: extending the C-l...

Both issues address the same core problem: extending the C-level iterator/generator protocol to support the `send()` functionality. Issue #2628 specifically requests extending `obj->iternext()` to accept a send argument for optimal C-level generator implementation. Issue #402 proposes a unified `mp_resume()` function that dispatches to `mp_iternext()` for C-level iterators and handles generator protocol with send/throw capabilities. These are describing the same underlying architectural need - the inability to send values into C-level iterators/generators. Issue #402 is the broader RFC that encompasses the specific request in #2628, and both reference the same code areas (py/obj, py/iter, iternext, send).

Action: Close issue #2628 as a duplicate of #402, as #402 provides the comprehensive RFC that addresses the same functionality with a more complete architectural proposal.

The PR implements the exact feature requested in the issue - a mechanism for native type inheritance by making the attribute lookup loop over parent types in the hierarchy. The issue requested support for inheritance in native types where .load_attr didn't check .bases_tuple, and this PR implements linear native inheritance by searching through parents of the type.

Action: Close issue #1159 as resolved by PR #4360

#6857 SPI issues with F7 impacted by IRQIssue #685 SPI.send() can get interruptedclosed0.304DUPLICATE
Both issues describe the exact same root problem: SPI transf...

Both issues describe the exact same root problem: SPI transfers on STM32 becoming unstable or stalling when interrupted by IRQs. Issue #6857 reports chaotic SPI communication that is resolved by manually disabling IRQs during transfers, while #685 explicitly requests DMA-based transfers to prevent interruption. The symptoms (unstable transfers, need for IRQ control), affected components (stm32/spi), and the underlying cause (IRQ interference with SPI transfers) are identical. Issue #6857 even references #1677 which is related to the same class of problems. The candidate issue (#685) represents the original feature request to solve this exact problem via DMA implementation.

Action: Close issue #6857 as a duplicate of #685. The core problem is the same: SPI transfers need protection from IRQ interruption, and #685 proposes the solution (DMA-based transfers). If #685 has been resolved with a merged PR, close #6857 as resolved by that PR.

The candidate PR #1511 directly addresses the root cause des...

The candidate PR #1511 directly addresses the root cause described in issue #1509. The issue reports that RTC resets to 2014 when waking from standby because backup register 0 is cleared by the tamper pin, causing rtc_init() to incorrectly detect a power-up. The PR explicitly states it 'Checks for year (RTC_TR) instead of backup register 0 to detect power up', which is the exact solution needed to fix the problem. The PR title 'Re issue #1509 RTC initialisation fix' confirms it was created to resolve this specific issue.

The PR adds a buffer length check to prevent this memory corruption and raises a ValueError instead of crashing. This is a direct resolution of the reported bug.

The PR #1038 directly addresses this exact problem with the title 'aiohttp: ws: fix truncated read of payload'. The PR description explains the root cause (read() doesn't guarantee reading all requested bytes) and provides a fix that loops until the expected length is received. Both items affect the same file (aiohttp_ws.py) and the same function (_read_frame). This is a clear case where a merged PR resolves the reported bug.

Action: Close issue #1037 as resolved by PR #1038

The issue #2415 was a tracking issue for Python 3.6 support, and this PR resolves one of the specific items (PEP 526) mentioned in that tracking issue. Since the PR implements the requested feature and references the original issue, this is a clear case of the issue being resolved by a merged PR.

Action: Close issue #2415 as resolved by PR #6126. Note that #2415 was a tracking issue for multiple Python 3.6 features; while PEP 526 is now implemented, other features listed in #2415 (like async generators PEP 525, async comprehensions PEP 530, etc.) may still be pending. Consider creating a new tracking issue for remaining Python 3.6 features if needed.

#762 webrepl: Support LAN as well as WLAN boards.Issue #11438 WebREPL on WIZnet W5500-EVB-Picoopen0.173DUPLICATE
Both items address the exact same underlying problem: webrep...

Both items address the exact same underlying problem: webrepl.py's setup_conn() function assumes network.WLAN exists, which fails on Ethernet-only boards (like the W5500-EVB-Pico in issue #11438). Issue #762 is a feature request to support network.LAN in addition to network.WLAN, while issue #11438 is a bug report describing the failure when only network.LAN is available. They describe the same root cause and the same solution: modify webrepl.py to handle both network interfaces gracefully. The candidate (#11438) fully addresses the query (#762) by identifying the problem that needs fixing.

Action: Close issue #11438 as a duplicate of #762 (or vice versa depending on which has better detail), as both describe the same issue requiring the same fix to webrepl.py to support network.LAN alongside network.WLAN.

#5046 RFC: MICROPY_PY_BUILTINS_NEXT2 always set to (0) ?Issue #3548 Built-in functon next() should be able to take a second optional argument.closed0.154DUPLICATE
Both issues address the same underlying problem: the lack of...

Both issues address the same underlying problem: the lack of support for the optional second argument (default value) in the `next()` built-in function in MicroPython. Issue #3548 requests the feature to allow `next(iterator, default)` to return a default value instead of raising StopIteration. Issue #5046 identifies that the configuration option `MICROPY_PY_BUILTINS_NEXT2` (which controls this feature) is always set to 0, breaking CPython compatibility. They describe the same missing functionality from different angles - one as a feature request, the other as a configuration bug preventing the feature.

Action: Close issue #5046 as a duplicate of #3548, as they address the same feature request. The configuration issue mentioned in #5046 is the root cause of the missing feature described in #3548.

#763 RFC: cleanup of sdcard.py codePR #765 SDcard sdcard.py rewrite for efficiency and functionopen0.130DUPLICATE
The issue #763 is an RFC requesting cleanup and performance ...

The issue #763 is an RFC requesting cleanup and performance improvements to sdcard.py, specifically mentioning adaptive timeout, proper CRC7 implementation on commands, and CRC16 support on data. The PR #765 directly addresses all these points: it rewrites sdcard.py for efficiency, implements mandatory CRC7 on all commands, adds optional CRC16 on data transfers, and reduces code size. The PR description explicitly states it went through the logic and found ways to make it faster and more reliable, which matches the RFC's goals. This is a clear case where a merged PR fully implements the requested feature.

Action: Close issue #763 as resolved by PR #765

#7769 How to set BLE connection interval?PR #7921 extmod/modbluetooth.c: Add connection interval to gap_connect.closed0.130DUPLICATE
The query (Issue #7769) asks how to set the BLE connection i...

The query (Issue #7769) asks how to set the BLE connection interval to save power, noting that advertisement/scan intervals are configurable but connection intervals are not. The candidate (PR #7921) explicitly adds `min_conn_interval_us` and `max_conn_interval_us` parameters to `BLE.gap_connect()`, directly addressing the user's need to configure connection intervals. The PR description confirms it solves the problem of MicroPython not having a way to obtain or set connection intervals. This is a feature implementation that resolves the user's request.

Action: Close Issue #7769 as resolved by PR #7921. The feature requested (setting connection interval) has been implemented in the PR.

The query (issue #1014) reports that the mip package install...

The query (issue #1014) reports that the mip package installer's _rewrite_url function doesn't handle GitLab repositories in subgroups, causing 403 errors when using the gitlab: shorthand syntax. The candidate (PR #816) explicitly implements support for installing from GitLab repositories by modifying the _rewrite_url() function to handle the gitlab: URL scheme. The PR description states 'Modify _rewrite_url() to allow mip install from gitlab:' which directly addresses the root cause identified in the issue. Both items concern the same component (micropython/mip/mip/__init__.py) and the same underlying problem (GitLab URL handling in mip).

Action: Close issue #1014 as resolved by PR #816. The PR implements the requested functionality to handle GitLab URLs in the _rewrite_url function, which will resolve the subgroup URL parsing issue described in the query.

The PR explicitly references issue #2415 in its description ('See #2415 for prior discussion on Python 3.6 support'). Issue #2415 is a tracking issue for Python 3.6 support that lists PEP 515 (Underscores in Numeric Literals) as one of the features to implement. This PR implements exactly that feature (PEP 515) and includes tests. The PR directly resolves the specific item marked as incomplete in the query issue.

Action: Close issue #2415 as resolved by PR #3831. Note that #2415 was a tracking issue for multiple Python 3.6 features; while this PR resolves the PEP 515 item, other items in the tracking issue may remain open. Consider updating the issue to reflect which features are still pending.

#8940 esp32: Heap fragmentation and mbedtls/lwip.PR #3533 py/gc: Support multiple heaps.closed0.087DUPLICATE
Issue #8940 describes ESP32 heap fragmentation problems wher...

Issue #8940 describes ESP32 heap fragmentation problems where MicroPython's single-heap allocation strategy fails to utilize fragmented IDF heap blocks, causing OOM errors during HTTPS requests. PR #3533 explicitly implements 'Support multiple heaps' to address this exact problem, stating 'The ESP32 has a fragmented heap so to use all of it the heap must be split.' The PR demonstrates successful use of multiple heap areas on ESP32 (200KB total across two areas), directly solving the fragmentation issue described in #8940. The PR's description even references the same IDF heap fragmentation pattern mentioned in #8940.

Action: Close issue #8940 as resolved by PR #3533. The PR implements the multiple heap support feature that directly addresses the fragmentation problem described in the issue.

#18663 Change to `mp_handle_pending' includes possible MICROPY_EVENT_POLL_HOOK and downstream code breakagePR #18672 py: clean up definition and use of `mp_handle_pending()`merged0.077DUPLICATE
PR #18672 explicitly references issue #18663 in its descript...

PR #18672 explicitly references issue #18663 in its description ("see #18663") and directly addresses the exact problem reported: the static-inline `mp_handle_pending()` change in commit c57aebf breaking external C modules and Zephyr port code. The PR fixes the undefined reference linker errors by replacing the bool/static-inline approach with an explicit enum API and removing incorrect extern declarations from mpconfigport.h files — precisely the remediation the issue called for.

Action: Close issue #18663 as resolved by PR #18672 once the PR is merged.

The PR explicitly states 'Closes: #18415' in its description, directly referencing the issue. The PR title 'stress_schedule: Fix for !GIL + emit=native' matches the issue's problem (stress_schedule failing with native emitter when GIL is disabled). The PR addresses the root cause identified in the issue - ensuring scheduled callbacks run during zero-duration sleeps, which was the missing behavior causing the test to fail.

Action: Close issue #18415 as resolved by PR #18423

#17521 docs: ESP32 RTC.memory and poweron_resetPR #12559 docs/library/machine: Add docs for RTC.memory() method.merged10.727LIKELY_DUPLICATE
Issue #17521 reports that the documentation for RTC.memory i...

Issue #17521 reports that the documentation for RTC.memory incorrectly claims it survives 'restarts' when it actually does not survive a poweron_reset (hardware reset). PR #12559 added documentation for RTC.memory() but the synopsis indicates it describes persistence across 'restarts' without clarifying the distinction between soft resets and hardware power-on resets. The PR likely introduced or perpetuated the inaccuracy that #17521 is reporting. However, #12559 is a PR that added the docs, while #17521 is a bug report about those docs being wrong. They are related to the same documentation error, but #12559 doesn't explicitly fix the issue - it may have caused it. The fix would need to be a separate PR that corrects the documentation to clarify the poweron_reset limitation.

Action: The issue #17521 should be kept open or referenced by a new PR that corrects the documentation to clarify that RTC.memory does not survive poweron_reset (hardware reset), only soft resets and deep sleep. PR #12559 added the documentation but did not address the accuracy issue.

The issue reports that ESP32 RTC.memory documentation incorrectly claims persistence across 'restarts' when it doesn't survive poweron_reset (EN button). The PR implements a feature to preserve RTC user memory across most resets (watchdog, software reset) but explicitly states that power-on reset (including via EN pin) will still clear the memory. The PR adds a compile-time flag to control this behavior. While the PR addresses the underlying technical limitation mentioned in the issue, it doesn't directly fix the documentation error - the documentation still needs to be updated to clarify that poweron_reset clears the memory. The PR provides the technical solution but leaves the documentation fix to be done separately.

Action: The issue should be kept open to track the documentation update. The PR resolves the technical capability question but the documentation still needs to be corrected to accurately reflect that poweron_reset clears RTC memory. Consider closing the issue as 'resolved by PR' only after the documentation is updated to match the new behavior.
