#1159 Inheritance for native types is not supportedPR #594 objtype: Don't treat inheritance from "object" as from native type.closed0.261RELATED
Issue #1159 requests support for inheritance in native types...

Issue #1159 requests support for inheritance in native types, specifically asking for .load_attr to check .bases_tuple for native types. PR #594 addresses a specific optimization in the type inheritance system (skipping lookups when inheriting from 'object') and modifies the same code area (py/objtype.c). However, PR #594 is a targeted optimization/refactor that doesn't implement the general native type inheritance feature requested in #1159. The PR improves the existing inheritance mechanism but doesn't provide the full solution for making native types truly inheritable with custom .load_attr methods as requested in the issue.

Action: Keep issue #1159 open as the general feature request for native type inheritance is not fully resolved by PR #594. The PR provides related improvements to the type system but doesn't address the core request for inheritance support in native types.

#18415 stress_schedule consistently fails with native emitterPR #17668 Allow the GIL to be enabled on the unix port, and add a CI job to test this configurationmerged0.259RELATED
PR #17668 modifies `tests/thread/stress_schedule.py` to add ...

PR #17668 modifies `tests/thread/stress_schedule.py` to add `time.sleep(0)` to busy-wait loops so it passes with the native emitter — directly overlapping with the symptom in issue #18415. However, the issue's root cause is in `py/scheduler.c` (commit 52a593c restricting scheduler callbacks to the main thread when GIL is disabled), which PR #17668 does not touch. The PR improves test robustness for native emitter mode and adds GIL-enabled CI coverage, but it doesn't fix the underlying scheduler logic that causes only 4 tasks to complete. The `time.sleep(0)` addition may mask or partially mitigate the issue on some configurations but does not address the bisected regression.

Action: Reference PR #17668 in the issue for context — it improves stress_schedule.py for native emitter but does not fix the scheduler.c behavior. The fix needs to address the interaction between the native emitter and the GIL-disabled scheduler callback restriction introduced in 52a593c. Ping @projectgus as the issue author already suggests.

#6332 Overflow converting -2147483648 to int when using MPZPR #17739 py/objint_longlong: Fix overflow check in mp_obj_int_get_checked.merged0.257RELATED
The query reports an overflow bug in the MPZ long integer im...

The query reports an overflow bug in the MPZ long integer implementation (MICROPY_LONGINT_IMPL_MPZ) when converting -2147483648 via mp_obj_get_int, and explicitly notes LONGLONG works correctly. The candidate PR fixes an overflow check in objint_longlong.c (LONGLONG implementation), not the MPZ implementation. They share the same error signature and concept domain (integer overflow in mp_obj_int_get_checked), but target different code paths. The PR does not fix the MPZ issue.

Action: Keep issue #6332 open — it reports an MPZ-specific bug that this LONGLONG-targeting PR does not address. Link the PR as related context when investigating the MPZ overflow check.

#2552 Add .offset()/.seek() method for memoryview for inplace "slicing"Issue #4331 Standardizing on an (extension) method to reinitialize an object inplaceopen0.257RELATED
Issue #4331 explicitly references #2552 as a related discuss...

Issue #4331 explicitly references #2552 as a related discussion point ('One case is being talked at https://github.com/micropython/micropython/issues/2552#issuecomment-262707357'). However, the two issues address different scopes: #2552 specifically requests adding .offset()/.seek() methods to memoryview for in-place slicing to avoid garbage creation. Issue #4331 proposes a broader standardization of an 'inplace reinitialization' method (like __reinit__) across multiple object types (memoryview, socket, uzlib) to enable object reuse. While #4331 mentions the memoryview use case from #2552, it proposes a different, more general solution (reinitialization vs. offsetting) and addresses a wider range of objects. The candidate does not resolve the specific feature request in #2552.

Action: Keep both issues open. #2552 remains a specific feature request for memoryview offset methods. #4331 is a broader RFC for object reinitialization protocols. They may inform each other but neither resolves the other.

Issue #4158 reports that the AP interface does NOT activate automatically on ESP32 when it should (for WebREPL). #8782 mpy_ld.py: "AttributeError: 'Symbol' object has no attribute 'section'" when relocating data sectionIssue #8783 mpy_ld.py: Unable to emit relocations in more complex .rodata.* sectionsclosed0.253RELATED

Both issues report bugs in mpy_ld.py related to linking with ESP-IDF compiler and handling ELF sections. However, they address different specific problems: Issue #8782 is about an AttributeError when relocating .data.rel.ro sections due to unresolved symbols missing the 'section' attribute, while Issue #8783 is about type errors when emitting relocations for complex .rodata.* sections like .rodata.str1.1 that don't match the existing section name checking logic. The fixes are in different functions (do_relocation_data vs build_mpy) and address different root causes. They are related as they both involve mpy_ld.py and ELF relocation handling with ESP-IDF, but neither resolves the other.

Action: Keep both issues open as they address distinct bugs in the same tool. Consider whether they might be part of a broader ESP-IDF compatibility effort, but treat them as separate issues requiring separate fixes.

#94 umqtt.simple publish with qos 1 pid rollover Issue #103 umqtt.simple socket behaviour when WiFi is degradedclosed0.248RELATED
Both issues concern the umqtt.simple library and QoS 1 messa...

Both issues concern the umqtt.simple library and QoS 1 message publishing, but they address fundamentally different problems. Issue #94 focuses on a specific bug where the packet ID (PID) overflows a 16-bit field and wraps to 0 (an illegal value per MQTT spec). Issue #103 focuses on socket blocking behavior and lack of timeouts when WiFi is degraded, causing hangs. While both involve QoS 1, the root causes and solutions are distinct: one is an integer overflow bug, the other is a network timeout/robustness issue.

Action: Keep both issues open as they address separate problems. Issue #94 requires a fix for PID rollover logic. Issue #103 requires implementing socket timeouts and handling PUBACK timeouts.

Issue #3998 is a bug report about a hard fault during boot on STM32F765 and related boards (NUCLEO_F767ZI, STM32F769DISC). Action: Keep both issues open. The bug in #3998 should be investigated as it may prevent successful deployment of STM32F765 support. Once the hard fault issue is resolved, #6281 can proceed with adding the board support files.

Issue #12611 is about filesystem directories shadowing frozen modules, making them inaccessible. While both involve frozen module visibility, the root causes and symptoms differ: #5495 is about symlink handling in the build/freeze process, while #12611 is about import resolution precedence between filesystem and frozen modules. The candidate does not resolve the query's specific issue with symlinks.

Action: Keep both issues open as they address distinct problems. The maintainer may want to review if there's any connection in the frozen module loading code, but they are not duplicates.

#1450 Single precision floating point formatting issuesIssue #11141 Wrong float numbers in print()closed0.245RELATED
Both issues concern floating-point formatting in MicroPython...

Both issues concern floating-point formatting in MicroPython and involve the same code component (py/objfloat.c). However, they address different specific problems: Issue #1450 is about format string syntax (e.g., '{:.4e}'.format(), '%5.g') producing incorrect output that doesn't match CPython's behavior (wrong precision, wrong notation choice, lack of normalization). Issue #11141 is about floating-point representation artifacts appearing during print() operations (e.g., 84.00001 instead of 84.0) due to IEEE 754 precision limitations. While both involve float-to-string conversion, the root causes and specific bugs are different - one is about format specifier handling, the other is about inherent floating-point precision issues during arithmetic operations.

Action: Keep both issues open as they address distinct problems. Issue #1450 should be resolved by fixing format string parsing and output generation in py/objfloat.c to match CPython behavior. Issue #11141 may require different solutions such as improved rounding in print() or documentation about floating-point precision limitations.

PR #5027 introduces the `esp32.Partition` class and enables mounting partitions via `uos.mount()`, which is the feature being attempted in the issue. However, PR #5027 does not address the specific bug that `find()` returns a list rather than a single object. The PR enables the functionality but doesn't fix the API inconsistency that causes the error in the issue. The issue requires a fix to the `find()` method's return type or usage pattern, which is not covered by this PR.

Action: Keep issue #9800 open. The PR #5027 enables the feature but doesn't resolve the specific bug reported. The issue should be reviewed to determine if `find()` should return a single object when one match is found, or if the documentation/example needs to be updated to handle the list return value.

Issue #17974 concerns the Pico W using the CYW43 WiFi driver (ports/rp2/cyw43_driver.c), where the hostname is not exposed in AP mode. #6572 NRF52840: ADC is 8 bits, how to set to 12 bits?PR #4213 docs: Specify new machine.ADC and machine.ADCBlock classesmerged0.217RELATED
The query requests ADC resolution configuration on NRF52840 ...

The query requests ADC resolution configuration on NRF52840 (8-bit vs 12-bit). The candidate PR proposes a standardised `machine.ADCBlock` class with a `bits` parameter that would directly enable resolution control, and the PR description explicitly mentions nrf as a target port. However, this is a docs-only spec proposal — it contains no implementation code. It does not resolve the query because the nrf ADC resolution feature was never actually implemented through this PR. The PR identifies the same gap and proposes a framework to address it, but leaves the issue open.

Action: Keep open. Reference PR #4213 as the proposed API direction for implementing ADC resolution on nrf. The issue can be closed once a PR implementing `ADCBlock` (or equivalent `bits` parameter) for the nrf port is merged.

#3715 stm32: Adding pins and peripherals to the board filesPR #16543 stm32: Support 'FDCAN' in board pin CSVs.merged0.217RELATED
Both items concern STM32 board pin configuration files (pins...

Both items concern STM32 board pin configuration files (pins.csv) and peripheral support. However, they address different specific problems: Issue #3715 is a feature request to add missing pin definitions and peripheral support (accelerometer, microphone, audio DAC, USB OTG, JTAG) to DISCO and NUCLEO boards. PR #16543 is a technical fix to automatically map FDCAN peripheral names to CAN during board pin processing. While both involve STM32 board files and peripheral configuration, the PR does not resolve the broader feature request in the issue about adding comprehensive pin/peripheral definitions for specific boards.

Action: Keep both items open. The PR addresses a specific CAN peripheral naming issue, while the issue requests a broader enhancement to add missing pin definitions and peripheral support for multiple boards.

#17222 esp32s3 builtin serial prompt: mpremote connects but cp/ls not workingIssue #7485 mpremote with mount fails on ESP32closed0.215RELATED
Both issues involve mpremote and ESP32, but they address dif...

Both issues involve mpremote and ESP32, but they address different problems. Issue #17222 is a feature request about custom USB device descriptors where mpremote connects but file management commands (cp/ls) fail due to the custom USB configuration. Issue #7485 is a bug report about mpremote failing during soft reset when a directory is mounted, causing PyboardError and EINVAL errors. The root causes are different: #17222 relates to USB descriptor configuration and file transfer protocol support, while #7485 relates to filesystem mount/unmount handling during soft reset. Neither resolves the other's problem.

Action: Keep both issues open as they address distinct problems. #17222 needs USB file transfer protocol implementation for custom descriptors, while #7485 needs fixing of the mount/unmount soft reset handling.

#7905 Discussion of Python 3.9 supportPR #6126 py: Implement PEP 526, syntax for variable annotationsmerged0.214RELATED
The query (Issue #7905) is a tracking issue for Python 3.9 f...

The query (Issue #7905) is a tracking issue for Python 3.9 features, including PEP 585 (type hinting generics in standard collections) and PEP 593 (flexible function and variable annotations). The candidate (PR #6126) implements PEP 526 (variable annotations syntax), which was introduced in Python 3.6, not 3.9. While both deal with type annotations and grammar changes, PR #6126 addresses a Python 3.6 feature that predates Python 3.9. The query specifically tracks 3.9 features, and PEP 526 is not one of them. The PR is related to the broader topic of type annotations in MicroPython but does not resolve the specific 3.9 feature tracking request.

Action: Keep both items open. The PR implements a pre-3.9 feature (PEP 526) that may be useful for compatibility, but it does not address the Python 3.9 specific features listed in the tracking issue.

#18868 tests: cmdline/repl_lock.py and repl_cont.py intermittent failuresPR #1467 py: Escape quotes are recognised correctly.closed0.214RELATED
The query (issue #18868) reports intermittent CI failures in...

The query (issue #18868) reports intermittent CI failures in REPL tests (repl_lock.py and repl_cont.py) due to timing issues on QEMU and quote escaping inconsistencies on macOS. The candidate (PR #1467) is an older PR that fixed REPL quote escaping logic in py/repl.c and added test coverage for repl_cont.py. While both items involve REPL quote handling and the repl_cont.py test, they address different problems: PR #1467 fixed a fundamental REPL parsing bug (issue #1419), whereas issue #18868 reports intermittent CI failures attributed to timing/emulation issues and platform-specific behavior. The PR does not resolve the intermittent CI failures described in the query.

Action: Keep issue #18868 open; the candidate PR addresses a related but distinct REPL parsing issue, not the intermittent CI failures reported.

#1681 stmhal: ADC class only uses ADC1 hardwareIssue #6219 [ESP32] Read from ADC2 Pinsclosed0.214RELATED
Both issues address the same core problem: ADC class only su...

Both issues address the same core problem: ADC class only supporting ADC1 hardware, leaving ADC2/ADC3 pins inaccessible. However, they affect different ports (STM32 vs ESP32) with different underlying causes. The STM32 issue (1681) is about hardware ADC instances on STM32F407, while the ESP32 issue (6219) is about ESP32's specific ADC2 limitations (WiFi conflict). While the symptoms and user frustration are similar, the root causes and solutions are port-specific. The candidate does not resolve the query as it addresses a different hardware platform.

Action: Keep both issues open as they represent port-specific ADC limitations. Consider cross-referencing them in comments to show users that ADC2/ADC3 support is a broader topic across ports, but each requires its own solution.

#16560 RingIO is limited to 64kBPR #9458 py/ringbuf: Add micropython.ringbuffer() interface for general use.merged0.213RELATED
PR #9458 introduced the RingIO feature (micropython.RingIO()...

PR #9458 introduced the RingIO feature (micropython.RingIO()) with the uint16_t-based ringbuf_t struct. Issue #16560 is a follow-on enhancement request to remove the 64kB limit by widening those fields to uint32_t. The issue explicitly cites PR #9458 as the origin of the limitation. PR #9458 does not address the size limit — it is the source of it. The candidate is the parent feature, not a fix for the query's problem.

Action: Leave issue #16560 open. It is a distinct enhancement request. Link it to PR #9458 for context. The fix would require a separate PR modifying ringbuf_t fields to uint32_t (or a configurable type).

#8017 SHA512 and hashlib portPR #61 hashlib: Fix shadowing of sha256 and sha512 constructors.closed0.212RELATED
The query (issue #8017) is a feature request to add SHA512 s...

The query (issue #8017) is a feature request to add SHA512 support to MicroPython's core hashlib module in the main micropython/micropython repository. The candidate (PR #61) is a bug fix in the micropython-lib repository that fixes a module shadowing issue preventing hashlib.sha512() from working in the library version. While both relate to SHA512 in hashlib, they address different repositories and different problems: the query asks for implementing SHA512 in the core C code, while the PR fixes a Python import issue in the library. The PR does not resolve the feature request in the main repository.

Action: Keep both items open. The query remains a valid feature request for the main repository, and the PR is a separate bug fix in the library repository.

#1700 Custom exception cannot be re-raised from called functionPR #390 vm: Save current active exception on opening new try block.merged0.211RELATED
Both issues concern bare `raise` failing to reraise the corr...

Both issues concern bare `raise` failing to reraise the correct exception, rooted in how the VM tracks the "active" exception. PR #390 fixes the case where a nested try block inside an except handler clobbers the saved exception, causing the wrong exception to be reraised. The query describes a distinct scenario: bare `raise` inside a called function fails entirely with "No active exception to reraise" — the exception context is not propagated across function call boundaries at all. The root cause is related (VM exception context tracking) but the failure mode and fix location differ. PR #390's test in `try-reraise2.py` does not cover the query's cross-function-call scenario, so the PR does not fully resolve the query.

Action: Keep both open. The query (issue #1700) documents a distinct bug — bare raise across function call boundaries — not covered by PR #390's fix. Reference #390 in the issue as prior related work on exception reraising semantics.

#1128 Performing operations on non-initialized Timer leads to crashes/lockupsPR #3923 stm32: add period_hz argument to timer constructor/initialiserclosed0.209RELATED
Issue #1128 reports a critical bug where Timer operations on...

Issue #1128 reports a critical bug where Timer operations on non-initialized timers cause crashes, lockups, and unexpected callback behavior. The core problem is improper state handling in the Timer implementation. PR #3923 adds a new 'period_hz' argument to the Timer constructor/initializer, which is a feature enhancement to the API. While both items affect the same component (ports/stm32/timer.c), PR #3923 does not address the fundamental bug of uninitialized Timer state causing crashes. The PR focuses on adding a new configuration option rather than fixing the state validation and initialization logic that causes the crashes described in #1128.

Action: Keep issue #1128 open. The PR #3923 is a feature addition that doesn't resolve the crash/lockup bug. The maintainer should investigate the Timer state initialization logic in ports/stm32/timer.c to ensure proper validation before operations on non-initialized timers.

#6980 Pyboard.py doesn't keep file path with folders on windowsPR #10821 tools/pyboard.py: Use '/' exclusively when dealing with paths.merged0.209RELATED
Both items concern Windows path handling bugs in tools/pyboa...

Both items concern Windows path handling bugs in tools/pyboard.py:filesystem_command(). The query reports that the source directory component is stripped when using shorthand destination notation (`:` instead of `:srptools/context.py`). The PR fixes a distinct but adjacent bug: `dest.endswith(os.path.sep)` returning False on Windows when destination ends in `/` because os.path.sep is `\`. The PR's fix (normalizing all paths to `/`) could incidentally affect how the cp destination is resolved, but the core issue in the query—preserving the source subdirectory in the destination—is about fname_cp_dest() logic, not just separator normalization. The PR does not explicitly reference issue #6980 or describe fixing path preservation for subdirectories.

Action: Leave open. PR #10821 addresses a related but distinct Windows path separator bug. The query's specific problem (source subdirectory stripped when using shorthand `:` destination) may still need a separate fix in the fname_cp_dest() logic. Verify whether #10821 also resolves this behavior after merge.

#1719 JSON module does not throw exception when object is not serialisablePR #863 Simple json printing using existing print framework.closed0.209RELATED
Issue #1719 reports a bug where MicroPython's JSON module si...

Issue #1719 reports a bug where MicroPython's JSON module silently accepts non-serializable bytes objects and produces corrupted output instead of raising a TypeError like CPython. PR #863 is an early implementation of JSON serialization functionality (the `_json` module with `dumps()`), which is the foundational work for the JSON module. However, PR #863 does not address the specific bug of proper type validation and exception handling for non-serializable objects. The PR description even states 'It doesn't handle non-json-able objects correctly'. While PR #863 is related to the JSON module and provides the base functionality, it does not resolve the specific bug reported in #1719 about bytes serialization and exception handling.

Action: Keep both items open. Issue #1719 needs a separate fix to add proper type validation and raise TypeError for non-serializable objects like bytes. PR #863 may have been merged as part of the initial JSON implementation, but the bug in #1719 requires additional work to handle edge cases properly.

#17236 rp2: Flash writes disrupt UART IRQIssue #16619 rp2: flash writes broken after starting core1 and performing a soft resetclosed0.207RELATED
Both issues concern flash write operations on the RP2 port, ...

Both issues concern flash write operations on the RP2 port, but they describe different root causes and symptoms. Issue #17236 is about UART IRQs being disabled during flash writes causing data loss. Issue #16619 is about flash writes hanging after a soft reset when core1 is active due to multicore lockout state not being reset. While both involve flash operations on RP2, the underlying problems are distinct: one is about interrupt handling conflicts with UART, the other is about multicore synchronization state persistence across resets. They share the 'rp2/flash' component but address different bugs.

Action: Keep both issues open as they address different problems. The maintainer may want to note the relationship in comments since both involve flash operations on RP2, but neither resolves the other.

The candidate (PR #7647) fixes a bug where listing serial ports fails when VID/PID are None and adds a 'devs' shortcut command. While both items relate to mpremote and serial port handling, the PR does not address the core request of the issue - it doesn't add environment variable support or config file options for selecting the default serial port. The PR fixes a different problem (formatting errors during listing) rather than implementing the requested feature.

Action: Keep both items open. The PR addresses a separate bug, while the issue remains a feature request that needs to be implemented separately.

Issue #8217 is about general SD card mounting failures on ESP32 S3, referencing issue #8121. While both involve SD cards on ESP32, the underlying problems are distinct - one is a filesystem API compatibility issue, the other is a driver/mounting issue.

While PR #4752 deals with factory reset code organization and file provisioning, it does not implement the core feature requested in the issue: making factory reset callable from Python code or providing a programmatic way to trigger a factory reset. The PR improves the infrastructure for factory reset but doesn't address the accessibility from code aspect that is the main request.

Action: Keep both items open. The PR improves factory reset code organization but does not resolve the feature request. The issue should remain open to track the implementation of programmatic factory reset functionality.

Issue #6996 proposes a two-level interrupt handler architecture to separate high-priority hardware ISRs from lower-priority MicroPython Pin ISRs to reduce latency for urgent external hardware. PR #9438 fixes a specific deadlock in lightsleep related to CYW43 WiFi interrupts and GPIO interrupt handling. While PR #9438 deals with GPIO interrupts and critical sections on RP2040, it addresses a different problem (WiFi lightsleep deadlock) rather than the general interrupt latency issue proposed in #6996. The PR does not implement the two-level interrupt handler architecture requested in the issue.

Action: Keep both items open. The PR addresses a specific bug related to WiFi interrupts and lightsleep, while the issue proposes a broader architectural change for interrupt handling that remains unimplemented.

Both items discuss I2S implementation in MicroPython, but th...

Both items discuss I2S implementation in MicroPython, but they address fundamentally different use cases and ports. Issue #8527 is a feature request for ESP32 to add I2S-driven ADC sampling (using I2S peripheral to capture ADC data via DMA). Issue #1165 is an RFC for STM32 to add a general I2S class for audio streaming (transmitting/receiving audio data to/from codecs). While both involve I2S peripherals and DMA, the specific functionality (ADC sampling vs audio codec streaming), target ports (ESP32 vs STM32), and implementation details are different. Action: Keep both issues open as they address different features on different ports. The STM32 I2S work may provide useful implementation patterns for the ESP32 I2SADC feature, but they are not duplicates.

They share the same underlying concept (incorrect pin mapping for I2C) but affect completely different hardware platforms, ports, and specific pins. #4437 STM32 UART buffer silent overrunIssue #1912 stmhal: UART doesn't override RTS when buffer gets fullclosed0.194RELATED

Issue #1912 is a bug report about hardware flow control (RTS) not being triggered when the buffer fills. The RTS fix in #1912 would help prevent overflow but doesn't address the core request in #4437 for circular buffer behavior or exception throwing.

Action: Keep both issues open. #1912 addresses flow control prevention of overflow, while #4437 requests different functionality (circular buffer, exceptions) that would require separate implementation.

#18531 Add function 'settimeout' to class SSLSocketPR #4219 unix/modusocket: Finish socket.settimeout() implementation.closed0.192RELATED
The query requests adding settimeout() and gettimeout() meth...

The query requests adding settimeout() and gettimeout() methods to the SSLSocket class in extmod/ssl. The candidate PR (#4219) implements socket.settimeout() for the unix port's regular socket implementation (ports/unix/modusocket.c). While this PR addresses the underlying socket timeout functionality, it does not implement the feature for SSLSocket specifically. SSLSocket is a wrapper around regular sockets that needs its own settimeout() implementation to delegate to the underlying socket. The PR fixes the base socket functionality but leaves the SSLSocket feature request open.

Action: Keep the issue open. The candidate PR implements the underlying socket.settimeout() functionality which is a prerequisite, but the SSLSocket class still needs its own settimeout() and gettimeout() methods implemented in extmod/ssl/ssl.c or similar. The issue should be reassessed after the base socket implementation is merged to determine if SSLSocket methods need to be added separately.

Both issues concern WiFi configuration persistence on ESP8266, but they address different specific problems. Issue #2569 is about the Access Point (AP) interface remaining active after reboot due to state persisted in flash by inisetup.py, which the user didn't explicitly configure. Issue #11546 is about documentation clarity regarding which STA_IF (Station) configuration parameters persist across reboots (hostname, country code, SSID/password). While both involve WiFi state persistence on ESP8266, #2569 focuses on an unwanted AP interface being active, whereas #11546 focuses on missing documentation about STA configuration persistence. The root causes and solutions are different.

#12048 STM32H5 machine.ADC core temperature sensor not workingIssue #1418 Wrong internal ADC valuesclosed0.182RELATED
Both issues concern incorrect internal ADC values (temperatu...

Both issues concern incorrect internal ADC values (temperature, VREF, VBAT) on STM32 ports, but they address different root causes on different hardware families. Issue #12048 is about STM32H5 where the channel number encoding (0xc3210000) is not being decoded via __LL_ADC_CHANNEL_TO_DECIMAL_NB() before configuring the SMPR register. Issue #1418 is about STM32F42x where the problem involves register bit conflicts (VBATE/TSVREFE) and insufficient sampling time for internal channels. While both involve STM32 ADC internal channels returning wrong values, the specific bugs, affected hardware, and required fixes are different.

Action: Keep both issues open as they address distinct problems. Issue #12048 requires fixing channel decoding for STM32H5, while #1418 requires register bit handling and sampling time adjustments for STM32F42x.

The root causes are different (thread safety vs. state tracking), and neither resolves the other's problem.

Action: Keep both issues open as they address distinct bugs. Consider linking them in comments to indicate they are related WiFi issues on the same platform but with different symptoms and root causes.

#18663 Change to `mp_handle_pending' includes possible MICROPY_EVENT_POLL_HOOK and downstream code breakagePR #17347 py/scheduler: Allow C scheduler callbacks to re-queue themselves.merged0.180RELATED
Both involve `mp_handle_pending` and the scheduler, but addr...

Both involve `mp_handle_pending` and the scheduler, but address distinct problems. The query (#18663) is about a linkage regression: `mp_handle_pending` was made inline in runtime.h, breaking external C modules that call it without that header. PR #17347 restructures the scheduler loop to prevent infinite loops when callbacks re-queue themselves — a behavioral/correctness fix with no bearing on the linkage/ABI issue the query describes.

Action: No action needed between these two items. The query (#18663) requires a separate fix — either reverting the inline change, providing a non-inline symbol, or updating documentation/examples for external module authors.

The PR adds a get_fifo_proxy() method exposing raw FIFO memory via the buffer protocol, enabling non-blocking access through direct memory reads rather than modifying the get/put API. The PR's approach satisfies the non-blocking need but does not implement the timeout semantics or the short-count return behavior the issue specifically asks for. Additionally, the PR does not appear to be merged, so it does not resolve the query regardless.

Action: Link the issue to PR #7650 as prior art for non-blocking FIFO access via a different mechanism (memory proxy). The issue's request for timeout semantics on get/put remains open and distinct. If #7650 is merged, note it as a partial alternative but keep the issue open for the timeout/MIN/TIME feature.

Issue #573 is the original feature request that introduced t...

While both issues concern the `const` optimization feature, they address different aspects - #573 requested the feature's creation, while #11929 requests a behavioral change to the existing feature. The candidate does not resolve the query; it provides historical context for the feature being discussed.

Action: Keep both issues open. Issue #573 is the original feature request (likely already resolved by the implementation). Issue #11929 is a separate enhancement request that proposes changing the behavior of `micropython.const` to raise an error when used incorrectly. The maintainer should evaluate whether to implement the proposed changes in #11929.

#18967 OpenMV Feature: Memory map supportIssue #18969 OpenMV Feature: Upstreaming patchesopen0.175RELATED
Both issues are from OpenMV regarding upstreaming their fork...

Both issues are from OpenMV regarding upstreaming their fork to MicroPython. Issue #18967 specifically requests 'memory map support' as a first step to replace custom DMA buffer fixes. Issue #18969 is a broader request to upstream approximately 40 commits from OpenMV's fork. While #18967 is a specific feature request that is mentioned as part of the upstreaming effort in #18969, the candidate issue (#18969) does not resolve the specific memory map feature request in the query. They are connected topics but address different scopes - one is a specific feature, the other is a general upstreaming effort.

Action: Keep both issues open. Issue #18967 should be tracked as a specific feature request for memory map support. Issue #18969 can serve as a meta-issue tracking the broader upstreaming effort, potentially referencing #18967 and other specific feature requests.

#1700 Custom exception cannot be re-raised from called functionIssue #12239 esp32/ble: Fatal crashes in BLE ISR handlerclosed0.174RELATED
Both issues deal with exception handling in MicroPython when...

Both issues deal with exception handling in MicroPython when exceptions are raised in called functions rather than directly in the handler. Issue #1700 reports that bare 'raise' statements fail to re-raise custom exceptions from within called functions (producing 'RuntimeError: No active exception to reraise'). Issue #12239 reports a similar symptom pattern where exceptions raised in called functions cause crashes in ESP32 BLE ISR context. However, they have different root causes: #1700 is about exception context tracking in the VM's exception handling mechanism (py/runtime, py/objexcept), while #12239 is specifically about NLR (non-local return) stack unwinding failures in interrupt context (py/nlr.c). The symptoms are similar (exceptions not properly propagated from called functions) but the underlying mechanisms and affected code areas differ significantly.

Action: Keep both issues open as they address different aspects of exception handling. Issue #1700 should be investigated for exception context tracking bugs, while #12239 should be investigated for NLR stack unwinding issues in interrupt contexts. They may share some common patterns but require separate fixes.

#1642 stm32: Implement Asynchronous UART TXIssue #3934 RFC: having a standard way to specify UART buffer lengthsclosed0.174RELATED
Issue #1642 requests asynchronous/non-blocking UART TX imple...

Issue #1642 requests asynchronous/non-blocking UART TX implementation (DMA/interrupt-driven) to prevent blocking during large writes. Issue #3934 discusses standardizing UART buffer length configuration (read_buf_len/write_buf_len) across ports. While both relate to UART buffer management on STM32, they address different aspects: #1642 focuses on the *mechanism* of non-blocking writes (DMA/interrupts), whereas #3934 focuses on *configuring buffer sizes* as a general API feature. The buffer size configuration in #3934 could support the async TX feature in #1642, but #3934 does not implement or resolve the core request for asynchronous TX behavior.

Action: Keep both issues open. #3934 may inform the implementation of #1642 by providing a standard API for buffer configuration, but #1642 requires separate work to implement the actual asynchronous TX mechanism.

#18935 stm32lib: ST not adding new parts.Issue #337 STM32CubeF4 - a panacea?closed0.173RELATED
Both issues concern STM32 driver support in MicroPython, but...

Both issues concern STM32 driver support in MicroPython, but they address different time periods and specific problems. Issue #18935 (2024) discusses the lack of support for new STM32 families (H5, C0, C5) in the unified stm32lib and the need to integrate standalone drivers or transition to HAL2. Issue #337 (2013) is an early proposal to adopt STM32CubeF4 as a replacement for the legacy peripheral driver library. While both relate to STM32 HAL/driver architecture, #337 is historical context about the initial HAL adoption, whereas #18935 is a current feature request for new hardware support. The candidate does not resolve the query's specific problem about integrating H5/C0/C5 drivers.

Action: Keep both issues open. #337 is historical context; #18935 requires active discussion about driver integration strategy for new STM32 families.

#15456 docs: explain optional global-interpreter-lockIssue #7662 ESP32: long hardware SPI transfers unnecessarily holding up other threadsclosed0.173RELATED
The query (issue #15456) is a documentation request asking f...

The query (issue #15456) is a documentation request asking for clarification on MicroPython's threading model, specifically whether a GIL exists by default, how native multithreading works on multicore systems, and how to implement mutex synchronization. The candidate (issue #7662) is a bug report about ESP32 SPI transfers not releasing the GIL, which demonstrates that a GIL exists and can block threads. While the candidate confirms the existence of a GIL and shows its impact on threading, it does not address the documentation request itself. The candidate provides technical context about GIL behavior but doesn't resolve the need for documentation explaining the threading model, GIL presence, or mutex usage.

Action: Keep both items open. The documentation request (#15456) should be addressed separately with documentation updates. The ESP32 SPI issue (#7662) is a valid bug report that may need its own fix.

#9203 port/ESP32/network.AP_IF: get IP addresses from connected stationsIssue #3906 RSSI of connected STAs from AP not workingopen0.166RELATED
Both issues concern the ESP32/ESP8266 network AP functionali...

Both issues concern the ESP32/ESP8266 network AP functionality and the 'stations' parameter of ap.status(). However, they address different specific features: Issue #9203 requests adding IP addresses to the station list (currently only MAC is available), while Issue #3906 reports that RSSI retrieval for connected stations is not working (ValueError). These are distinct feature requests for different data fields (IP vs RSSI) in the same API. The candidate does not resolve the query's request for IP address support.

Action: Keep both issues open as they represent different feature requests. The candidate issue provides context about the 'stations' parameter implementation but does not address the IP address requirement.

#18415 stress_schedule consistently fails with native emitterPR #15448 py/scheduler,rp2: Avoid scheduler race conditions when GIL disabled, fix "TinyUSB callback can't recurse" errormerged0.164RELATED
PR #15448 introduced commit 52a593cdb, which is the exact co...

PR #15448 introduced commit 52a593cdb, which is the exact commit the query issue bisected as the first bad commit. The PR's change — restricting scheduler callbacks to the main thread when GIL is disabled — is the root cause of the regression reported in #18415. However, PR #15448 does not fix the native emitter stress_schedule failure; it caused it. The query describes a new bug introduced by this PR's behavioral change, not a problem the PR resolves.

Action: Link #18415 as a regression introduced by PR #15448. The issue needs a separate fix — either adjusting how the main-thread-only scheduler constraint interacts with the native emitter, or revisiting the scheduling policy for native-emitted code.

#5371 reversed() does not work as expected for ucollections.OrderedDictIssue #2156 OrderedDict doesn't test for equalityclosed0.164RELATED
Both issues concern ucollections.OrderedDict in the same com...

Both issues concern ucollections.OrderedDict in the same component (extmod/collections), but they address different bugs. Issue #5371 is about the reversed() builtin not working correctly with OrderedDict (iteration protocol/__reversed__ method), while issue #2156 is about equality comparison (__eq__) being broken. These are distinct functionality problems with different root causes and fixes.

Action: Keep both issues open as they address different bugs. Do not mark as duplicates.

#8614 Can't distinguish generator type from coroutine type in esp32 Micropython 1.17Issue #2184 unix: type of generator function is incorrectclosed0.164RELATED
Both issues concern type introspection of generators in Micr...

Both issues concern type introspection of generators in MicroPython, but they address different problems. Issue #8614 reports that coroutines (async functions) are incorrectly reported as 'generator' type, making it impossible to distinguish between coroutines and generators. Issue #2184 reports that generator *functions* (the callable objects) are incorrectly reported as 'generator' type instead of 'function'. While both involve the 'generator' type classification, #8614 is about coroutines being misidentified as generators, whereas #2184 is about generator functions being misidentified as generator instances. These are distinct bugs in different code areas (coroutine type vs generator function type).

Action: Keep both issues open as they address different problems. Issue #8614 should be investigated separately to determine if coroutines need their own type or if the current implementation conflates them with generators.

#8782 mpy_ld.py: "AttributeError: 'Symbol' object has no attribute 'section'" when relocating data sectionPR #17377 tools/mpy_ld.py: support absolute relocations in text section on Thumb targetsmerged0.163RELATED
Both touch `tools/mpy_ld.py:do_relocation_data` and `do_relo...

Both touch `tools/mpy_ld.py:do_relocation_data` and `do_relocation_text`, but address distinct problems. The query reports an AttributeError caused by an unresolved (UND) symbol lacking a `.section` attribute on Xtensa/ESP-IDF. PR #17377 adds R_ARM_ABS32 support in the text section for ARM Thumb targets and enables soft-float on armv6m/armv7m. The candidate does not introduce the `hasattr(s, "resolved")` guard the query proposes and does not fix the reported AttributeError.

Action: Keep the query issue open. The maintainer may want to cross-reference #17377 as prior art for how resolved-symbol handling was approached in the same functions, but the specific UND-symbol AttributeError on Xtensa remains unfixed.

#18626 Missing MP_BINARY_OP_REVERSE_LESS etc.PR #11478 py: Improve support for inplace operatorsmerged0.161RELATED
Both items touch binary operator dispatch in py/runtime.c an...

Both items touch binary operator dispatch in py/runtime.c and deal with reverse/reflected operator support. However, they address distinct gaps: the query requests new MP_BINARY_OP_REVERSE_LESS/MORE/EQUAL/etc. enum entries and dispatch logic for comparison operators, while PR #11478 improves inplace operator fallback (e.g. __iadd__ → __add__) and moves reverse arithmetic operator support from objtype.c to runtime.c for all types. The candidate does not add reverse comparison operators to mp_binary_op_t, which is the core request in the query. The architectural change in #11478 (moving fallback logic to runtime.c) is relevant context for implementing the query's fix, but leaves the comparison operator gap open.

Action: Reference PR #11478 as prior art for the architectural pattern (reverse operator fallback in runtime.c). The query requires a separate, targeted change to add MP_BINARY_OP_REVERSE_LESS through MP_BINARY_OP_REVERSE_NOT_EQUAL to the mp_binary_op_t enum and dispatch logic — this work is not covered by #11478.

#17544 Make ROMFS Bootable.PR #16857 Define `vfs.rom_ioctl()`, add romfs commands to mpremote, and implement ROM partition support on stm32, rp2, esp32, esp8266merged0.161RELATED
PR #16857 implements the foundational ROMFS infrastructure: ...

PR #16857 implements the foundational ROMFS infrastructure: vfs.rom_ioctl(), automatic mounting at /rom, and port-specific ROM partition drivers. The PR summary mentions sys.path integration, meaning imports from /rom likely work once mounted. However, the query specifically requests that ROMFS be searched during boot execution (boot.py/main.py via pyexec_file_if_exists()) and that ROMFS be prioritized in the import search order relative to frozen scripts — neither of which is addressed by this PR. The PR is a prerequisite that makes bootable ROMFS possible, but the bootability feature itself remains unimplemented.

Action: Keep issue #17544 open as a distinct feature request. Reference PR #16857 as the foundational infrastructure that enables this work, and note that the remaining implementation — executing boot.py/main.py from ROMFS and inserting ROMFS into the import search path relative to frozen modules — still needs to be done.

#16604 Emitted code epilogue block contains a superflous jump to the next available code address.PR #15310 py: fix native emitter to work with `async with` statementsmerged0.161RELATED
PR #15310 introduced the `-march=debug` native emitter that ...

PR #15310 introduced the `-march=debug` native emitter that the query issue (#16604) uses to demonstrate the superfluous jump bug. The PR's own debug output example even shows the same pattern (`jump(label_0)` immediately before `label(label_0)`), confirming the issue predates #16604 but was not addressed by #15310. The PR fixes `async with` support and adds the debug emitter, both touching `py/emitnative.c`, but it does not eliminate the redundant epilogue jumps. The query bug remains open.

Action: Keep issue #16604 open. Reference PR #15310 as the commit that introduced the debug tooling used to observe this bug. The superfluous jump optimisation needs a separate fix in the epilogue label/jump emission logic in py/emitnative.c.

#18660 mpremote REPL and mount issues with socket:// and RFC2217 connectionsPR #11467 tools/mpremote: Improve speed of echoing device data to console.merged0.159RELATED
The query (issue #18660) reports critical bugs in mpremote w...

The query (issue #18660) reports critical bugs in mpremote with socket:// and RFC2217 connections: missing 'in_waiting' property causing mount failures, incorrect newline handling causing 'staircase' output, and race conditions causing REPL lag/hangs. The candidate (PR #11467) is a performance optimization that changes the REPL to read/write in chunks instead of single bytes to reduce CPU usage. While both items affect the mpremote REPL component, PR #11467 does not address the specific bugs reported in #18660 (missing property, newline handling, race conditions). The PR focuses on performance optimization rather than fixing the functional bugs. The issue explicitly lists commits that fix the reported problems (aeb1ce6, 2d46d52, 7f0abfd, 1ff555c), none of which appear to be PR #11467.

Action: Keep both items open. PR #11467 is a performance improvement that is unrelated to the functional bugs in #18660. The maintainer should ensure the specific bug-fix commits referenced in #18660 are merged to resolve the reported issues.

#525 Method Resolution Order (MRO) is not compliantIssue #8648 [question] Comparing a callable with a function that gets promoted to a method during the instantiation of a classopen0.158RELATED
The candidate is a question about namespace sharing across p...

The candidate is a question about namespace sharing across parent/child classes in MicroPython's inheritance model, and the user explicitly mentions MRO constraints as a possible explanation. However, the core issue is about dynamic attribute propagation through shared class namespaces — a distinct mechanism from C3 linearization. The query is specifically about depth-first vs C3 MRO for method lookup ordering; the candidate is about whether modifications to a base class after instantiation affect child instances. These touch the same subsystem (py/objtype, attribute resolution) but describe different behaviors.

Action: No action needed — keep both open. The candidate may be partially explained or resolved if #525 is fixed, but it raises a separate concern about namespace sharing that warrants its own investigation.

#2620 RFC: offical API to save WiFi-connection parameterIssue #6747 Wifi credentials are not store to flashclosed0.158RELATED
Both issues address WiFi credential persistence across Micro...

Both issues address WiFi credential persistence across MicroPython ports, but they focus on different aspects. Issue #2620 is an RFC discussing the design of an official API to control WiFi parameter saving, addressing flash wear-out concerns on ESP8266 and proposing various API approaches. Issue #6747 is a specific bug/feature request for ESP32 where WiFi credentials are not being stored to flash because the required esp_wifi_set_storage() and esp_wifi_set_auto_connect() calls are missing from modnetwork.c. While #6747 relates to the same general topic (WiFi persistence), it doesn't resolve the broader API design questions raised in #2620, nor does it address the flash wear-out concerns. #6747 is more about implementing missing functionality for ESP32 rather than designing a unified API.

Action: Keep both issues open. #2620 should remain as the RFC for API design discussion, while #6747 should be addressed as a specific implementation gap for ESP32. The solution to #6747 may inform the final API design in #2620, but they are not duplicates.

#10963 Block device `readblock(8, 44)` before `ioctl(1)` for `os.mount(block_device, ...)`PR #7755 os.rst: Clarify littlefs requirements.closed0.156RELATED
Issue #10963 reports a bug where os.mount() calls readblock(...

Issue #10963 reports a bug where os.mount() calls readblock(8, 44) before ioctl(1), violating the documented block device interface contract. PR #7755 is a documentation update clarifying littlefs requirements regarding ioctl calls, specifically mentioning that ioctl(6, block) is issued before writeblocks(). While both items concern block device initialization and ioctl calls, PR #7755 only addresses documentation for littlefs and doesn't fix the actual bug in the mount sequence where readblock is called before ioctl(1). The PR provides context about ioctl usage but doesn't resolve the reported issue.

Action: Keep issue #10963 open. The documentation update in PR #7755 is helpful context but doesn't fix the underlying bug. Consider creating a new PR to fix the mount sequence to call ioctl(1) before readblock(8, 44) as documented.

#11025 Firmware 1.19.1 on NUCLEO-WB55 is failing on BLE functionsIssue #6316 [NUCLEO_WB55 - USBDONGLE_WB55] disconnect usbclosed0.154RELATED
Both issues concern BLE functionality on STM32WB55 boards (N...

Both issues concern BLE functionality on STM32WB55 boards (NUCLEO-WB55) and involve the STM32WB BLE stack firmware. However, they describe different problems: Issue #11025 reports a timeout error (tl_ble_wait_resp: timeout) when running BLE scripts after STMicroelectronics firmware updates, while Issue #6316 reports USB device disconnection from PC when running BLE examples. The root causes and symptoms are different - one is a BLE communication timeout, the other is a USB enumeration issue. They share the same hardware platform and BLE stack context but address distinct bugs.

Action: Keep both issues open as they address different problems. The maintainer may want to review both to understand if there's a common underlying cause in the STM32WB55 BLE/USB integration, but they should not be closed as duplicates.

Both issues concern the same test file (tests/extmod/ussl_ba...

Issue #11346 reports that calling close() twice produces double 'ioctl 4 0' output, which the .exp file doesn't expect. Issue #4364 reports that the test fails when using modussl_mbedtls because it's hardcoded for modussl_axtls behavior. While they share the same test file and component, they address different problems: #11346 is about close() idempotence and test expectations for a specific output pattern, while #4364 is about test compatibility between different SSL backends (axtls vs mbedtls). The root causes and specific issues are different, though they both relate to the same test file's behavior across different implementations.

Action: Keep both issues open as they address different problems. Issue #11346 should be investigated for close() idempotence behavior, while #4364 (if not already resolved) should address making the test compatible with both SSL backends.

#17242 wiznet5k_send_ethernet: fatal error -5Issue #17127 Unable to setup IP with nic.ipconfig() while works with obsolete nic.ifconfig() network.WIZNET5Kopen0.153RELATED

Issue #17127 reports that the new `ipconfig()` method fails to actually configure the network for ICMP pings, while the deprecated `ifconfig()` works. These are distinct bugs: one is about an error message during activation, the other is about configuration method behavior. They share the same component (extmod/network/wiznet5k) but address different root causes.

Action: Keep both issues open as they address different problems. The maintainer may want to investigate if there's a common underlying cause in the WIZNET5K driver changes between v1.23.0 and v1.24.0.

Both issues concern WiFi problems on the RP2040 Pico W port,...

Both issues concern WiFi problems on the RP2040 Pico W port, but they describe different root causes and symptoms. Issue #11977 is about a specific deadlock/hang when initializing an Access Point on core1 (multicore concurrency issue at ap.active(True)). Issue #16482 is about WiFi connection reliability, power management modes (PM_POWERSAVE vs PM_NONE), and false positive connection states during idle periods. While both involve the CYW43 driver and network functionality, they address distinct problems: one is a multicore synchronization bug, the other is a power management/reliability issue.

Action: Keep both issues open as they address different problems. The maintainer may want to review if there are any underlying shared causes in the CYW43 driver that could affect both scenarios, but they should be tracked separately.

#6635 real and imag raise exceptions on non-complex numbersPR #7623 py/objcomplex: Allow strings of the form -A+Bj.closed0.150RELATED

Issue #6635 requests adding .real and .imag attributes to non-complex numbers (int, float) for Python compatibility. PR #7623 implements parsing of complex number strings with both real and imaginary parts (e.g., '1+2j'). While both items involve complex numbers and CPython compatibility, they address completely different functionality: #6635 is about attribute access on int/float, while #7623 is about string parsing for complex literals. The PR does not resolve the issue's request.

Action: Keep both items open as they address different features. The PR may be useful context for future complex number enhancements but does not solve the reported problem.

#6718 urandom.randint handling of >32bit values on 64-bit archPR #13556 Fix compilation warnings for Windows 64 bitsmerged0.149RELATED
Both items involve 64-bit integer handling in extmod/modrand...

Both items involve 64-bit integer handling in extmod/modrandom.c, specifically in yasmarang_randbelow and mod_random_randint. However, the query reports a runtime behavioral bug (returns 0 or hangs for values >=2^32 due to implicit uint32_t truncation), while the candidate PR only adds type casts to suppress compilation warnings from 64-bit type mismatches. Adding casts to silence warnings does not fix the underlying algorithmic problem — the PRNG still operates on uint32_t internally, so values >32 bits will still be incorrectly handled at runtime. The candidate touches the same code but does not resolve the query's reported bug.

Action: Reference PR #13556 as context when fixing issue #6718, but treat the issue as still open. The actual fix requires either a 64-bit Yasmarang implementation or an explicit range check in randint/randrange to reject arguments exceeding uint32_t max on 64-bit platforms.

Both issues report bugs in the umqtt.simple library on the P...

Both issues report bugs in the umqtt.simple library on the Pico W platform, but they describe different symptoms and root causes. Issue #994 is about message transmission stalling when payloads exceed ~125 characters (outbound publishing issue), while Issue #951 is about the library stopping reception of broker responses after receiving a subscribed message (inbound processing issue). They share the same component (umqtt.simple) and platform (Pico W), but address different failure modes - one is a transmission/buffer limit problem, the other is a receive loop/callback handling problem. Neither resolves the other.

Action: Keep both issues open as they represent distinct bugs in the umqtt.simple library. Consider cross-referencing them in comments since they may share underlying network buffer or socket handling issues.

The root causes and symptoms are distinct - one is a threading/synchronization problem, the other is a configuration/implementation bug. Neither issue resolves the other.

Action: Keep both issues open as they address different bugs. The maintainer may want to review if there's any underlying common cause in the network/WLAN implementation that could be affecting both issues.

The candidate (PR #17955) addresses a different problem: making viper pointer tests handle low-memory conditions gracefully by pre-compiling code blocks and catching MemoryError during setup. While both involve test robustness and the candidate modifies some viper-related tests (which are also mentioned in the query), the root causes and solutions are different. The query is about exception message formatting changes affecting string comparisons, while the PR is about memory allocation timing and test skipping mechanisms. The PR does not resolve the issue of exception message format changes.

Action: Keep both items open. The PR addresses a separate test robustness issue (low-memory handling) and does not fix the exception message formatting problem described in the query.

The candidate (PR #12086) is a bug fix that addresses SPI construction issues for standard SPI modes (SPI1, SPI2, SPI3) on various ESP32 variants. While both items relate to ESP32 SPI functionality and affect the same code area (ports/esp32/machine_hw_spi.c), the PR does not implement or address the QSPI/Octal SPI feature request. The PR fixes existing SPI construction bugs but does not add the multi-line SPI capabilities requested in the issue.

Action: Keep both items open. The PR resolves SPI construction bugs but does not implement the QSPI/Octal SPI feature. The feature request should remain open for future implementation.

#2552 Add .offset()/.seek() method for memoryview for inplace "slicing"PR #4150 py/objarray: Introduce "memview_offset" alias for "free" field of the…closed0.143RELATED
The query (issue #2552) requests adding a new .offset()/.see...
